target: Future studies, to see which glucose measuring test is best in children, are unlikely to happen.

source: Thus, accepting the major ADA diagnostic criteria appears to be the best course of action for the pediatric community.
target: Accepting the ADA guidelines for diagnosing diabetes in children appears to be the best option for pediatricians.

source: One area in which correlation studies between HbA1c and FPG or OGTT might shed light is in the definition of criteria for intervention in 'pre-diabetes,' as the Diabetes Prevention Program Trial did not use HbA1c.
target: Future studies on blood glucose tests should try to develop a guideline for doctors to treat ‘pre-diabetes’.

source: However, such treatment, and the exact diagnostic thresholds at which it should be initiated in children, remains unproven.
target: However, such treatment, and the best way to diagnose and treat ’pre-diabetes’, has not been proven.

source: Diabetes mellitus is a common disease whose complications are severe.
target: Diabetes mellitus is a common disease whose side effects are severe.

source: For decades, the diagnosis of diabetes and prediabetes was using only fasting glucose or glucose two hours during an oral glucose tolerance test.
target: For decades, the diagnosis of diabetes and prediabetes was using only fasting (without food) glucose or glucose two hours during an by-mouth (oral) glucose sensitivity test.

source: Recently, it is possible to use HbA1c.
target: Recently, it is possible to use HbA1c (a simple blood sugar test).

source: Each of these tests has advantages and limitations that must be well known by clinicians for better care for patients.
target: Each of these tests has pros and cons that must be well known by clinicians for better care for patients.

source: So they could use one, two or three of this tests to reach to a proper diagnosis.
target: So they could use one, two or three of this tests to reach a proper diagnosis.

source: The aim of this article is about the strong and weak points of these tests.
target: This article's aim is about the strong and weak points of these tests.

source: Objective: The objective of this study was to compare the use of hemoglobin A1C to oral glucose tolerance testing to diagnose overt type 2 diabetes in the first trimester of pregnancy.
target: The objective of this study was to compare the use of hemoglobin A1C (a simple blood sugar test) to oral (by-mouth) glucose sensitivity (tolerance) testing to diagnose before-birth type 2 diabetes in the first trimester of pregnancy.

source: The study used a nonexperimental descriptive design to compare the use of the hemoglobin A1C test results to oral glucose tolerance test results.
target: The study used a nonexperimental design to compare the use of the hemoglobin A1C test results to oral glucose tolerance test results.

source: Methods: The study used a sample of 45 women at high risk for type 2 diabetes in the first trimester of pregnancy.
target: The study's methods included a sample of 45 women at high risk for type 2 diabetes in the first trimester of pregnancy.

source: Participants were consented to draw a hemoglobin A1C with their ordered oral glucose tolerance testing for comparison of the two tests' ability to diagnose overt type 2 diabetes.
target: Participants volunteered to draw a hemoglobin A1C with their oral glucose tolerance testing for comparison of the two tests' ability to diagnose type 2 diabetes.

source: Results: Hemoglobin A1C tests were highly positively correlated with oral glucose tolerance testing for diagnosis of type 2 diabetes in women in the first trimester of pregnancy.
target: For the study's results, hemoglobin A1C tests were highly linked with oral glucose tolerance testing for diagnosis of type 2 diabetes in women in the first trimester of pregnancy.

source: Conclusion: The research provides beginning evidence that the hemoglobin A1C should be considered as a first-tier diagnostic test for overt type 2 diabetes in the first trimester of pregnancy.
target: In conclusion, the research provides beginning evidence that the hemoglobin A1C should be considered as an important diagnostic test for before-birth type 2 diabetes in the first trimester of pregnancy.

source: Introduction: Maturity onset diabetes of the young (MODY) is a rare form of monogenic diabetes.
target: The study's introduction states that the maturity onset diabetes of the young (MODY) is a rare form of single-gene-causing diabetes.

source: Being clinically and genetically heterogeneous, it is often misdiagnosed as type 1 or type 2 diabetes, leading to inappropriate therapy.
target: Being clinically and genetically diverse, it is often misdiagnosed as type 1 or type 2 diabetes, leading to inappropriate therapy.

source: MODY is caused by a single gene mutation.
target: MODY is caused by a single gene mutation.

source: Thirteen genes, defining 13 subtypes, have been identified to cause MODY.
target: Thirteen genes, defining 13 types, have been identified to cause MODY.

source: A correct diagnosis is important for the right therapy, prognosis, and genetic counselling.
target: A correct diagnosis is important for the right therapy, recovery, and genetic counselling.

source: Material and methods: Twenty-nine unrelated paediatric patients clinically suspected of having MODY diabetes were analysed using TruSight One panel for next-generation sequencing (NGS) and multiplex ligation-dependent probe amplification (MLPA) assay.
target: The study's material and methods include twenty-nine unrelated child patients clinically suspected of having MODY diabetes analysed with DNA sequencing.

source: Results: In this study we identified variants in MODY genes in 22 out of 29 patients (75.9%).
target: As the results in this study, we identified different types in MODY genes in 22 out of 29 patients (75.9%).

source: Using two genetic tests, NGS and MLPA, we detected both single nucleotide variants and large deletions in patients.
target: Using two genetic tests termed NGS and MLPA, we detected both small mutation types and large gene deletions in patients.

source: Most of the patients harboured a variant in the GCK gene (11/22), followed by HNF1B (5/22).
target: Most patients had a different type in the GCK gene (11/22), followed by HNF1B (5/22).

source: The rest of the variants were found in the NEUROD1 and HNF1A genes.
target: The rest of the different types were found in the NEUROD1 and HNF1A genes.

source: We identified one novel variant in the GCK gene: c.596T>C, p.Val199Ala.
target: We identified one novel gene type in the GCK gene.

source: The applied genetic tests excluded the suspected diagnosis of MODY in two patients and revealed variants in other genes possibly associated with the patient's clinical phenotype.
target: The applied genetic tests excluded the suspected diagnosis of MODY in two patients and revealed subtypes in other genes possibly linked with the patient's clinical physical characteristics.

source: Conclusions: In our group of MODY patients most variants were found in the GCK gene, followed by variants in HNF1B, NEUROD1, and HNF1A genes.
target: In conclusion, in our group of MODY patients, most gene types were found in the GCK gene, followed by changes in HNF1B, NEUROD1, and HNF1A genes.

source: The combined NGS and MLPA-based genetic tests presented a comprehensive approach for analysing patients with suspected MODY diabetes and provided a successful differential diagnosis of MODY subtypes.
target: The combined genetic tests presented a comprehensive way to analyze patients with possible MODY diabetes and provided a successful diagnosis of MODY subtypes.

source: Polyuria-polydipsia syndrome consists of the three main entities: central or nephrogenic diabetes insipidus and primary polydipsia.
target: Polyuria-polydipsia syndrome consists of the three main effects: central or nephrogenic (kidney-related) diabetes insipidus (constantly-urinating) and primary polydipsia (great thirst).

source: Reliable distinction between these diagnoses is essential as treatment differs substantially, with the wrong treatment potentially leading to serious complications.
target: Reliable differences between these diagnoses is needed as treatment differs greatly, with the wrong treatment potentially leading to serious side effects.

source: Past diagnostic measures using the classical water deprivation test had several pitfalls and clinicians were often left with uncertainity concerning the diagnosis.
target: Past identification measures using the classical water deprivation test had several pitfalls and clinicians were often left with uncertainity regarding the diagnosis.

source: With the establishment of copeptin, a stable and reliable surrogate marker for arginine vasopressin, diagnosis of the polyuria-polydipsia syndrome has been newly evaluated.
target: With the creation of copeptin, a stable and reliable surrogate marker for arginine vasopressin (blood vessel constriction), diagnosis of the polyuria-polydipsia syndrome has been newly evaluated.

source: Whereas unstimulated basal copeptin measurement reliably diagnoses nephrogenic diabetes insipidus, two new tests using stimulated copeptin cutoff levels showed a high diagnostic accuracy in differentiating central diabetes insipidus from primary polydipsia.
target: Whereas unstimulated basal copeptin (specific protein) measurement reliably identifies nephrogenic diabetes insipidus, two new tests using stimulated copeptin cutoffs showed a high identification accuracy in differentiating central diabetes insipidus from primary polydipsia.

source: For the hypertonic saline infusion test, osmotic stimulation via the induction of hypernatraemia is used.
target: For the hypertonic (very salty) saline infusion test, water-sucking stimulation via a high salt amount is used.

source: This makes the test highly reliable and superior to the classical water deprivation test, but also requires close supervision and the availability of rapid sodium measurements to guarantee the safety of the test.
target: This makes the test highly reliable and superior to the classical water deprivation test, but also requires close watch and the availability of rapid sodium measurements to guarantee the test's safety.

source: Alternatively, arginine infusion can be used to stimulate copeptin release, opening the doors for an even shorter and safer diagnostic test.
target: Alternatively, arginine infusion (adding a specific protein in) can be used to stimulate copeptin release, opening the doors for an even shorter and safer diagnostic test.

source: The test protocols of the two tests are provided and a new copeptin-based diagnostic algorithm is proposed to reliably differentiate between the different entities.
target: The test protocols of the two tests are provided and a new copeptin-based diagnostic method is proposed to reliably identify between the different entities.

source: Furthermore, the role of copeptin as a predictive marker for the development of diabetes insipidus following surgical procedures in the sellar region is described.
target: Furthermore, the role of copeptin as a predictive marker for the development of diabetes insipidus following surgeries in the sellar region (an area of the head) is described.

source: The classification of diabetes was originally limited to only two categories called juvenile-onset diabetes mellitus, now known as type 1 diabetes mellitus, and adult-onset diabetes mellitus, now known as type 2 diabetes mellitus.
target: Classifying diabetes was originally limited to either juvenile-onset diabetes mellitus, or type 1 diabetes mellitus, and adult-onset diabetes mellitus, or type 2 diabetes mellitus.

source: This has grown to a recognition of more than 50 subcategories caused by various pathogenic mechanisms or accompanying other diseases and syndromes.
target: Classification of diabetes now recognizes over 50 subcategories by disease-causing biological processes or accompanying diseases.

source: The diagnosis of diabetes has evolved from physician recognition of typical symptoms to detection of ambient hyperglycemia and, thence, to the definition of excessive plasma glucose levels after an overnight fast and/or following challenge with a glucose load (oral glucose tolerance test or OGTT), and more recently, by measurement of glycated hemoglobin (A1c).
target: Diagnosing diabetes has grown from a physician recognizing typical signs to detecting high blood sugar to defining high blood sugar after an overnight fast and/or tolerance test with glucose (a simple sugar). Recently, the diagnosis involves measuring glycated hemoglobin (A1c), an iron-rich protein in blood joined to a sugar.

source: Screening has uncovered a much higher prevalence of diabetes in the United States and elsewhere, as well as its enormous public health impact.
target: Testing now shows higher widespreadness of and enormous public health impact of diabetes in the United States and elsewhere.

source: Modern testing has defined individuals at high risk for the development of diabetes and pregnant women whose fetuses are at increased risk for mortality and morbidity.
target: Current testing has detected those at risk for diabetes and pregnant women whose fetuses are at higher risk for death and illness.

source: Diagnostic glycemic criteria for presymptomatic diabetes have been set using diabetic retinopathy as a specific complication of the disease: A1c ≥6.5%; fasting plasma glucose (FPG) ≥126 mg/dL; or plasma glucose measured 2 hours after an OGTT (2-hour PG) ≥200 mg/dL.
target: Diagnostic glycemic (blood sugar) criteria for before-symptom diabetes have been set using diabetic retinopathy (eye-affecting diabetes) as a specific effect of the disease: A1c ≥6.5%; fasting plasma glucose (FPG) ≥126 mg/dL; or blood glucose (sugar) measured 2 hours after an OGTT (2-hour PG) ≥200 mg/dL.

source: For patients with typical symptoms, a random plasma glucose ≥200 mg/dL is diagnostic.
target: For patients with typical symptoms, a random blood glucose ≥200 mg/dL is diagnostic.

source: The 2-hour PG yields the highest prevalence and A1c the lowest.
target: The 2-hour PG (a type of blood sugar measurement) yields the highest prevalence and A1c (another test for diabetes) the lowest.

source: A1c is the most convenient and practical test, requiring no preparation, is analytically superior, and has the lowest intraindividual variation.
target: A1c is the most convenient and practical test, needing no preparation, is analytically betterr, and has the lowest variation.

source: It is more expensive than the FPG, but the same or less than the OGTT.
target: It is more expensive than the FPG (a diabetes test), but the same or less than the OGTT (a diabetes test).

source: The 2-hour PG is the most burdensome to the patient and has the highest intraindividual variation.
target: The 2-hour PG is the most burdensome to the patient and has the highest variation.

source: Standardized measurement of A1c is not available everywhere.
target: Comparable measurement of A1c is not available everywhere.

source: Confirmation of an abnormal test with the same test is recommended.
target: Checking an abnormal test with the same test is recommended.

source: Studies in various populations show inconsistency among the glycemic tests.
target: Studies in various groups show inconsistency among the glycemic tests.

source: Of people meeting the A1c criterion, 27%–98% meet plasma glucose criteria.
target: Of people meeting the A1c criterion, 27%–98% meet plasma glucose criteria.

source: Of people meeting plasma glucose criteria, 17%–78% meet the A1c criterion.
target: Of people meeting plasma glucose criteria, 17%–78% meet the A1c criterion.

source: These discrepancies occur because each test measures different aspects of hyperglycemia that may vary among patients.
target: These discrepancies occur because each test measures different aspects of hyperglycemia (high blood sugar) that may vary among patients.

source: While the risk of future diabetes is continuously associated with plasma glucose and A1c, the areas between the upper limits of normal and the diabetes cutpoints have been called “prediabetes” or “high risk for diabetes.” These have been defined categorically as A1c 6.0%–6.4% or 5.7%–6.4%; impaired fasting glucose (IFG), FPG 100–125 mg/dL; and impaired glucose tolerance (IGT), 2-hour PG 140–199 mg/dL. A1c 6.0%–6.4% increases the odds ratio (OR) for progression to diabetes (OR 12.5–16) more than the range of 5.7%–6.4% (OR 9.2).
target: While the risk of future diabetes is continuously associated with plasma glucose and A1c, the areas between the upper limits of normal and the diabetes cutpoints have been called “prediabetes” or “high risk for diabetes.” These have been defined categorically as A1c 6.0%–6.4% or 5.7%–6.4%; impaired fasting glucose (IFG), FPG 100–125 mg/dL; and impaired glucose tolerance (IGT), 2-hour PG 140–199 mg/dL. A1c 6.0%–6.4% increases the odds ratio (OR) for progression to diabetes (OR 12.5–16) more than the range of 5.7%–6.4% (OR 9.2).

source: In U.S. studies, the incidence of type 2 diabetes averages approximately 6% per year in people with IGT and can reverse spontaneously.
target: In U.S. studies, the incidence of type 2 diabetes averages approximately 6% per year in people with IGT and can reverse spontaneously.

source: IFG is more prevalent than IGT in the United States, though IGT rises more sharply with age.
target: IFG is more prevalent than IGT in the United States, though IGT rises more sharply with age.

source: IFG increases the risk of future diabetes to various degrees in different countries, with odds ratios ranging from 2.9 to 18.5.
target: IFG increases the risk of future diabetes to various degrees in different countries, with odds ratios ranging from 2.9 to 18.5.

source: The proper treatment of hyperthyroidism depends on recognition of the signs and symptoms of the disease and determination of the etiology.
target: Successfully treating an overactive thyroid depends on identifying the signs and symptoms of the disease and determining the cause.

source: The most common cause of hyperthyroidism is Graves' disease.
target: Graves' disease, a disease in which infection-preventing cells attack healthy cells, is the most common cause of an overactive thyroid.

source: Other common causes include thyroiditis, toxic multinodular goiter, toxic adenomas, and side effects of certain medications.
target: Other common causes of an overactive thyroid include thyroid inflammation, one or more glandular growths making extra thyroid hormone, and side effects of some medications.

source: The diagnostic workup begins with a thyroid-stimulating hormone level test.
target: A common test that measures blood levels of hormones that acts as messengers to the thyroid is the first step in a medical exam.

source: When test results are uncertain, measuring radionuclide uptake helps distinguish among possible causes.
target: When results of this test are uncertain, a test to measure thyroid function can be used.

source: When thyroiditis is the cause, symptomatic treatment usually is sufficient because the associated hyperthyroidism is transient.
target: When the cause is inflammation of the thyroid, treating the symptoms is enough because the overactive thyroid is short-lived.

source: Graves' disease, toxic multinodular goiter, and toxic adenoma can be treated with radioactive iodine, antithyroid drugs, or surgery, but in the United States, radioactive iodine is the treatment of choice in patients without contraindications.
target: Graves' disease and when one or more growths make extra thyroid hormone can be treated with radiation therapy, drugs that block the formation of thyroid hormone, or surgery. In the United States, radiation therapy to shrink the thyroid is preferred unless there is a reason not to do so.

source: Thyroidectomy is an option when other treatments fail or are contraindicated, or when a goiter is causing compressive symptoms.
target: Thyroid removal is an option when other treatments do not work or should not be used, or when an enlarged thyroid is causing pressure or squeezing.

source: Some new therapies are under investigation.
target: New treatments are being studied.

source: Special treatment consideration must be given to patients who are pregnant or breastfeeding, as well as those with Graves' ophthalmopathy or amiodarone-induced hyperthyroidism.
target: People who are pregnant or breastfeeding, and people with thyroid eye disease or overactive thyroids caused by amiodarone, a heart medication, must be given special treatment consideration.

source: Patients' desires must be considered when deciding on appropriate therapy, and dose monitoring is essential.
target: We must factor in what patients want when deciding on treatment. Maintaining a safe and effective dose is very important.

source: Thyroiditis is a general term that encompasses several clinical disorders characterized by inflammation of the thyroid gland.
target: Thyroiditis refers to medical conditions that include thyroid inflammation.

source: The most common is Hashimoto thyroiditis; patients typically present with a nontender goiter, hypothyroidism, and an elevated thyroid peroxidase antibody level.
target: The most common type of thyroiditis is Hashimoto thyroiditis. Patients usually have a painless enlarged thyroid, underactive thyroid, and high levels of proteins made by the immune system to fight foreign substances.

source: Treatment with levothyroxine ameliorates the hypothyroidism and may reduce goiter size.
target: Taking levothyroxine, a thyroid drug, improves underactive thyroid and may decrease thyroid size.

source: Postpartum thyroiditis (occurring within one year of childbirth, miscarriage, or medical abortion) can be short-lived or long-lasting.
target: Short-lived or long-lasting thyroiditis can occur postpartum, within one year of childbirth, miscarriage, or medical abortion.

source: Release of preformed thyroid hormone into the bloodstream may result in hyperthyroidism.
target: Overactive thyroid can be caused by stored thyroid hormone released to the blood.

source: This may be followed by transient or permanent hypothyroidism as a result of depletion of thyroid hormone stores and destruction of thyroid hormone-producing cells.
target: Overactive thyroid may be followed by short-term or permanent underactive thyroid.

source: Patients should be monitored for changes in thyroid function.
target: Patients should be checked for changes in thyroid function.

source: Beta blockers can treat symptoms in the initial hyperthyroid phase; in the subsequent hypothyroid phase, levothyroxine should be considered in women with a serum thyroid-stimulating hormone level greater than 10 mIU per L, or in women with a thyroid-stimulating hormone level of 4 to 10 mIU per L who are symptomatic or desire fertility.
target: Beta blockers can improve overactive thyroid symptoms. Levothyroxine, a thyroid drug, can improve underactive thyroid symptoms in women with high blood levels of hormones that act as a messenger to the thyroid.

source: Subacute thyroiditis is a transient thyrotoxic state characterized by anterior neck pain, suppressed thyroid-stimulating hormone, and low radioactive iodine uptake on thyroid scanning.
target: Subacute thyroiditis is short-lived and characterized by neck pain, low blood levels of hormones that acts as a messenger to the thyroid, and low thyroid function based on a common test.

source: Many cases of subacute thyroiditis follow an upper respiratory viral illness, which is thought to trigger an inflammatory destruction of thyroid follicles.
target: Many subacute thyroiditis cases follow colds.

source: In most cases, the thyroid gland spontaneously resumes normal thyroid hormone production after several months.
target: The thyroid usually returns to normal on its own after many months.

source: Treatment with high-dose acetylsalicylic acid or nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs is directed toward relief of thyroid pain.
target: High-dose aspirin or similar over-the-counter pain relievers can be taken to relieve thyroid pain.

source: Thyroiditis is a general term that encompasses several clinical disorders characterized by inflammation of the thyroid gland.
target: Thyroiditis covers multiple disorders represented by inflammation of the thyroid gland.

source: The most common is Hashimoto thyroiditis; patients typically present with a nontender goiter, hypothyroidism, and an elevated thyroid peroxidase antibody level.
target: The most common disorder is Hashimoto thyroiditis. Patients have enlarged thyroids, reduced thyroid function, and increased proteins targeting thyroid products.

source: Treatment with levothyroxine ameliorates the hypothyroidism and may reduce goiter size.
target: Levothyroxine (a thyroid hormone treatment) improves thyroid function and size.

source: Postpartum thyroiditis (occurring within one year of childbirth, miscarriage, or medical abortion) can be short-lived or long-lasting.
target: Post-birth thyroiditis is temporary or lasting thyroid dysfunction occuring within a year of childbirth, miscarriage, or medical abortion.

source: Release of preformed thyroid hormone into the bloodstream may result in hyperthyroidism.
target: Supplemental thyroid hormone release into the blood may cause excess thyroid function.

source: This may be followed by transient or permanent hypothyroidism as a result of depletion of thyroid hormone stores and destruction of thyroid hormone-producing cells.
target: Temporary or permanent reduced thryoid function may follow excess thyroid function due to thyroid hormone depletion and destruction of hormone-producing cells.

source: Patients should be monitored for changes in thyroid function.
target: Patients should be monitored for changes in thyroid function.

source: Beta blockers can treat symptoms in the initial hyperthyroid phase; in the subsequent hypothyroid phase, levothyroxine should be considered in women with a serum thyroid-stimulating hormone level greater than 10 mIU per L, or in women with a thyroid-stimulating hormone level of 4 to 10 mIU per L who are symptomatic or desire fertility.
target: Beta blockers (common high blood pressure medication) can treat symptoms of the initial phase of excess thyroid function. Levothyroxine should be considered for later phases in women with high thyroid-stimulating hormone levels.

source: Subacute thyroiditis is a transient thyrotoxic state characterized by anterior neck pain, suppressed thyroid-stimulating hormone, and low radioactive iodine uptake on thyroid scanning.
target: Subacute or fairly rapid thyroiditis (thyroid inflammation) is a temporary, thyroid-hormone-excess state with frontal neck pain, reduced thyroid-stimulating hormone, and low iodine sensitivity.

source: Many cases of subacute thyroiditis follow an upper respiratory viral illness, which is thought to trigger an inflammatory destruction of thyroid follicles.
target: Fairly rapid thyroiditis often follows an upper airway viral illness, thought to trigger inflammatory destruction of thyroid cells.

source: In most cases, the thyroid gland spontaneously resumes normal thyroid hormone production after several months.
target: In most cases, the thyroid gland suddenly returns to normal after several months.

source: Treatment with high-dose acetylsalicylic acid or nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs is directed toward relief of thyroid pain.
target: High-dose aspirin or nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs help give thyroid pain relief.

source: Hyperthyroidism is an excessive concentration of thyroid hormones in tissues caused by increased synthesis of thyroid hormones, excessive release of preformed thyroid hormones, or an endogenous or exogenous extrathyroidal source.
target: Overactive thyroid is when too much thyroid hormone produced, when too much stored thyroid hormone released, or when there are internal or external thyroid hormone sources outside the thyroid.

source: The most common causes of an excessive production of thyroid hormones are Graves disease, toxic multinodular goiter, and toxic adenoma.
target: The most common causes of too much thyroid hormone made are Graves disease, a disease in which infection-preventing cells attack healthy cells and results in an overactive thyroid, or when one or more glandular growths make extra thyroid hormone.

source: The most common cause of an excessive passive release of thyroid hormones is painless (silent) thyroiditis, although its clinical presentation is the same as with other causes.
target: The most common causes of too much thyroid hormone released is thyroid inflammation, although the signs are the same as other causes.

source: Hyperthyroidism caused by overproduction of thyroid hormones can be treated with antithyroid medications (methimazole and propylthiouracil), radioactive iodine ablation of the thyroid gland, or surgical thyroidectomy.
target: Overactive thyroid can be treated with drugs that block the formation of thyroid hormone, radiation therapy, or surgery to remove the thyroid.

source: Radioactive iodine ablation is the most widely used treatment in the United States.
target: Radiation therapy is the most widely used treatment in the United States.

source: The choice of treatment depends on the underlying diagnosis, the presence of contraindications to a particular treatment modality, the severity of hyperthyroidism, and the patient's preference.
target: The choice of treatment depends on the diagnosis, reasons not to use a particular method, how serious the overactive thyroid is, and what the patient wants.

source: Background: Hashimoto's thyroiditis is an autoimmune disorder and the most common cause of hypothyroidism.
target: Hashimoto's thyroiditis is caused by thyroid cells that are attacked by infection-preventing cells and is the most common cause of an underactive thyroid.

source: The use of Nigella sativa, a potent herbal medicine, continues to increase worldwide as an alternative treatment of several chronic diseases including hyperlipidemia, hypertension and type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM).
target: More people around the world are taking Nigella sativa, a powerful herbal medicine, as a nontraditional way to treat many long-term diseases including high cholesterol, high blood pressure and type 2 diabetes.

source: The aim of the current study was to evaluate the effects of Nigella sativa on thyroid function, serum Vascular Endothelial Growth Factor (VEGF) - 1, Nesfatin-1 and anthropometric features in patients with Hashimoto's thyroiditis.
target: The current study aimed to rate the effects of Nigella sativa on how well the thyroid is working, blood Vascular Endothelial Growth Factor (VEGF) - 1 (a protein that promotes new blood vessels), Nesfatin-1 (a chain of amino acids that affects hunger) and physical body measurements in people with Hashimoto's thyroiditis.

source: Methods: Forty patients with Hashimoto's thyroiditis, aged between 22 and 50 years old, participated in the trial and were randomly allocated into two groups of intervention and control receiving powdered Nigella sativa or placebo daily for 8 weeks.
target: We randomly split forty people with Hashimoto's thyroiditis and between 22 and 50 years old into two group and gave one group powdered Nigella sativa and the other group sham treatment for 8 weeks.

source: Changes in anthropometric variables, dietary intakes, thyroid status, serum VEGF and Nesfatin-1 concentrations after 8 weeks were measured.
target: We measured changes in physical body measurements, what people ate, thyroid function, blood VEGF and Nefastin-1 levels after 8 weeks.

source: Results: Treatment with Nigella sativa significantly reduced body weight and body mass index (BMI).
target: Taking Nigella sativa caused lower body weight and body mass index (BMI).

source: Serum concentrations of thyroid stimulating hormone (TSH) and anti-thyroid peroxidase (anti-TPO) antibodies decreased while serum T3 concentrations increased in Nigella sativa-treated group after 8 weeks.
target: People taking Nigella sativa had lower blood levels of thyroid-stimulating hormone and antibodies that target the thyroid and higher levels of blood thyroid hormone (T4) after 8 weeks.

source: There was a significant reduction in serum VEGF concentrations in intervention group.
target: People taking Nigella sativa had lower blood levels of VEGF.

source: None of these changes had been observed in placebo treated group.
target: None of these changes were seen in the group who took the sham treatmentl.

source: In stepwise multiple regression model, changes in waist to hip ratio (WHR) and thyroid hormones were significant predictors of changes in serum VEGF and Nesgfatin-1 values in Nigella sativa treated group (P < 0.05).
target: Based on a statistical model, changes in the ratio of the waist to the hip and thyroid hormones predicted changes in blood VEGF and Nesgfatin-1 levels in the group who took Nigella sativa.

source: Conclusions: Our data showed a potent beneficial effect of powdered Nigella sativa in improving thyroid status and anthropometric variables in patients with Hashimoto's thyroiditis.
target: We concluded that powdered Nigella sativa improved thyroid function and physical body measurements in people with Hashimoto's thyroiditis.

source: Moreover, Nigella sativa significantly reduced serum VEGF concentrations in these patients.
target: Nigella sativa lowered blood VEGF levels.

source: Considering observed health- promoting effect of this medicinal plant in ameliorating the disease severity, it can be regarded as a useful therapeutic approach in management of Hashimoto's thyroiditis.
target: Nigella sativa can be a useful non-traditional treatment for people with Hashimoto's thyroiditis to make the disease less severe.

source: Background: Hashimoto's thyroiditis is an autoimmune disorder and the most common cause of hypothyroidism.
target: Hashimoto's thyroiditis (thyroid inflammation) occurs when immune cells mistakenly attack the body's own healthy cells. It is the most common cause of reduced thyroid function.
