target: Severe hypercalcemia, or too high calcium blood levels, is rare in newborns. Even though hypercalcemia often shows no symptoms, it may have important effects on overall health.

source: Hypophosphatemia can occur in infants experiencing intrauterine malnutrition, sepsis and early high-energy parenteral nutrition (PN) and can cause severe hypercalcemia through an unknown mechanism.
target: Hypophosphatemia is when the body has low levels of phosphorous. Hypophosphatemia can occur in infants experiencing malnutrition while in the womb, blood infection, and early high-energy parenteral nutrition (PN). PN is when a mother is given nutrients through IV. Hypophosphatemia can cause severe hypercalcemia through an unknown mechanism.

source: Monitoring and supplementation of phosphate (PO4) and calcium (Ca) in the first week of life in preterm infants are still debated.
target: It is debated if phosphate (PO4) and calcium (Ca) should be given in the first week of life in preterm infants.

source: Case presentation: We report on a female baby born at 29 weeks' gestation with intrauterine growth retardation (IUGR) experiencing sustained severe hypercalcemia (up to 24 mg/dl corrected Ca) due to hypophosphatemia while on phosphorus-free PN.
target: This study reports on a female baby born at 29 weeks' gestation (time in womb). The infant had intrauterine growth retardation (IUGR) and long-term severe hypercalcemia due to hypophosphatemia while on phosphorus-free PN. IUGR is when a baby does not grow to normal weight during pregnancy.

source: Hypercalcemia did not improve after hyperhydration and furosemide but responded to infusion of PO4.
target: Hypercalcemia did not improve after excess water intake but responded to infusion of PO4.

source: Eventually, the infant experienced symptomatic hypocalcaemia (ionized Ca 3.4 mg/dl), likely exacerbated by contemporary infusion of albumin.
target: Eventually, the infant experienced symptoms of hypocalcemia. This was likely worsened by infusion of albumin, a blood protein.

source: Subsequently, a normalization of both parathyroid hormone (PTH) and alkaline phosphatase (ALP) was observed.
target: Following this, parathyroid hormone (PTH), which controls calcium and phosphorus in the blood, and alkaline phosphatase (ALP), which removes phosphate groups,  returned to normal levels.

source: Conclusions: Although severe hypercalcemia is extremely rare in neonates, clinicians should be aware of the possible occurrence of this life-threatening condition in infants with or at risk to develop hypophosphatemia.
target: Severe hypercalcemia is extremely rare in newborns. However, doctors should be aware of the possible occurrence of this life-threatening condition in infants with or at risk to develop hypophosphatemia.

source: Hypophosphatemic hypercalcemia can only be managed with infusion of PO4, with strict monitoring of Ca and PO4 concentrations.
target: Hypophosphatemic hypercalcemia can only be managed with infusion of PO4, with strict monitoring of Ca and PO4 concentrations.

source: Background: Hypophosphatasia (HPP) is an inborn disease caused by pathogenic variants in ALPL.
target: Hypophosphatasia (HPP) is a disease caused by mutations within the gene ALPL. Hypophosphatasia (HPP) is the heritable, tooth and bone disease. The gene ALPL encodes for alkaline phosphatase (ALP), an enzyme that helps bone strength.

source: Low levels of alkaline phosphatase (ALP) are a biochemical hallmark of the disease.
target: Low levels of alkaline phosphatase (ALP) are a hallmark of the disease.

source: Scarce knowledge about the prevalence of HPP in Scandinavia exists, and the variable clinical presentations make diagnostics challenging.
target: There is little knowledge about the prevalence of HPP in Scandinavia. Due to the variation in HPP clinical presentations, diagnosing the disease is challenging.

source: The aim of this study was to investigate the prevalence of ALPL variants as well as the clinical and biochemical features among adults with endocrinological diagnoses and persistent hypophosphatasaemia.
target: The aim of this study was to investigate the prevalence of ALPL variants (gene types). The study also aimed to identify HPP biological identifiers among adults. This study was specifically interested in adults with HPP along with endocrine (hormonal) disorders and persistent hypophosphatasemia (low body phosphorus levels).

source: Methods: A biochemical database containing ALP measurements of 26,121 individuals was reviewed to identify adults above 18 years of age with persistently low levels of ALP beneath range (≤ 35 ± 2.7 U/L).
target: A database containing ALP measurements of 26,121 individuals was reviewed to identify adults above 18 years of age with persistently low levels of ALP.

source: ALPL genetic testing, biochemical evaluations and assessment of clinical features by a systematic questionnaire among included patients, were performed.
target: ALPL genetic testing, evaluation of body compound levels, and assessment of health by a systematic questionnaire among included patients, were performed.

source: Results: Among 24 participants, thirteen subjects (54.2%) revealed a disease-causing variant in ALPL and reported mild clinical features of HPP, of which musculoskeletal pain was the most frequently reported (n = 9).
target: Among 24 participants, thirteen subjects revealed a disease-causing variant in ALPL and reported mild health effects of HPP. Of these mild health effects, musculoskeletal pain was the most frequently reported.

source: The variant c. 571G > A; p.(Glu191 Lys) was identified in six subjects, and an unreported missense variant (c.1019A > C; p.(His340Pro)) as well as a deletion of exon 2 were detected by genetic screening.
target: The same genetic variant was identified in six subjects. An unreported missense (gene-altered) variant and a genetic deletion were detected by genetic screening.

source: Biochemical analyses showed no significant differences in ALP (p = 0.059), the bone specific alkaline phosphatase (BALP) (p = 0.056) and pyridoxal-5'-phosphate (PLP) (p = 0.085) between patients with an ALPL variant and negative genetic screening.
target: Analysis of compound levels within the body showed no significant differences between patients with an ALPL variant and negative (no-detection) genetic screening.

source: Patients with a variant in ALPL had significantly higher PLP levels than healthy controls (p = 0.002).
target: Patients with a variant in ALPL had significantly higher PLP levels than control patients.

source: We observed normal ALP activity in some patients classified as mild HPP, and slightly increased levels of PLP in two subjects with normal genetic screening and four healthy controls.
target: Normal ALP activity was observed in some patients classified as mild HPP. A slightly increased level of PLP was found in two subjects with normal genetic screening and four healthy controls.

source: Among 51 patients with persistent hypophosphatasaemia, fifteen subjects (29.4%) received antiresorptive treatment.
target: Among 51 patients with persistent hypophosphatasemia, 15 received treatment to increase bone strength.

source: Two patients with unrecognized HPP were treated with bisphosphonates and did not show complications due to the treatment.
target: Two patients with undiagnosed HPP were treated with bisphosphonates, drugs that slow bone loss. They did not show complications due to the treatment.

source: Conclusions: Pathogenic variants in ALPL are common among patients with endocrinological diagnoses and low ALP.
target: Genetic variations in ALPL are common among patients with endocrine disorders and low ALP.

source: Regarding diagnostics, genetic testing is necessary to identify mild HPP due to fluctuating biochemical findings.
target: Genetic testing is necessary to identify mild HPP due to fluctuating body compound level findings.

source: Antiresorptive treatment is a frequent reason for hypophosphatasaemia and effects of these agents in adults with a variant in ALPL and osteoporosis remain unclear and require further studies.
target: Treatment to increase bone strength is a frequent reason for hypophosphatasemia. Effects of these agents in adults with a variant in ALPL and osteoporosis (brittle bones) remain unclear and require further studies.

source: Background: Hypophosphatasia (HPP) is an inborn disease caused by pathogenic variants in ALPL.
target: Low levels of blood phosphate, a high-energy molecule, or hypophosphatasia (HPP) is a from-birth disease caused by disease-causing variations in ALPL (the gene for alkaline phosphatase or a protein that influences phosphate levels).

source: Low levels of alkaline phosphatase (ALP) are a biochemical hallmark of the disease.
target: Low levels of alkaline phosphatase (ALP) are a marker of HPP.

source: Scarce knowledge about the prevalence of HPP in Scandinavia exists, and the variable clinical presentations make diagnostics challenging.
target: Little is known about the amount of HPP in Scandinavia. The diverse symptoms make identification challenging.

source: The aim of this study was to investigate the prevalence of ALPL variants as well as the clinical and biochemical features among adults with endocrinological diagnoses and persistent hypophosphatasaemia.
target: The study aimed to investigate the amount of ALPL variants and physical and biochemical markers among adults with hormonal diagnoses and persistant low ALP levels.

source: Methods: A biochemical database containing ALP measurements of 26,121 individuals was reviewed to identify adults above 18 years of age with persistently low levels of ALP beneath range (≤ 35 ± 2.7 U/L).
target: We reviewed a database of ALP measurements from 26,121 individuals to identify adults over 18 years with persistently low levels of ALP.

source: ALPL genetic testing, biochemical evaluations and assessment of clinical features by a systematic questionnaire among included patients, were performed.
target: ALPL gene testing, biological tests, and assessment of symptoms by a questionnaire among included patients were performed.

source: Results: Among 24 participants, thirteen subjects (54.2%) revealed a disease-causing variant in ALPL and reported mild clinical features of HPP, of which musculoskeletal pain was the most frequently reported (n = 9).
target: Among 24 patients, thirteen (54.2%) had a disease-causing variant in ALPL and mild symptoms of HPP, of which musculoskeletal pain was the most frequent.

source: The variant c. 571G > A; p.(Glu191 Lys) was identified in six subjects, and an unreported missense variant (c.1019A > C; p.(His340Pro)) as well as a deletion of exon 2 were detected by genetic screening.
target: A specific gene mutation that altered a protein segment was found in six subjects. A similar gene mutation and deletion of a gene segment was detected by genetic tests.

source: Biochemical analyses showed no significant differences in ALP (p = 0.059), the bone specific alkaline phosphatase (BALP) (p = 0.056) and pyridoxal-5'-phosphate (PLP) (p = 0.085) between patients with an ALPL variant and negative genetic screening.
target: Tests showed no differences in ALP, bone-specific ALP, and pyridoxal phosphate (PLP) or active vitamin B6 between patients with an ALPL mutation and no genetic diseases.

source: Patients with a variant in ALPL had significantly higher PLP levels than healthy controls (p = 0.002).
target: Patients with an ALPL mutation had much higher PLP levels than healthy patients.

source: We observed normal ALP activity in some patients classified as mild HPP, and slightly increased levels of PLP in two subjects with normal genetic screening and four healthy controls.
target: We saw normal ALP activity in some patients with mild HPP. We also saw slightly increased PLP in two subjects with no gene diseases and four healthy patients.

source: Among 51 patients with persistent hypophosphatasaemia, fifteen subjects (29.4%) received antiresorptive treatment.
target: Among 51 with persistent low ALP levels, fifteen (29.4%) had anti-bone-breakdown treatment.

source: Two patients with unrecognized HPP were treated with bisphosphonates and did not show complications due to the treatment.
target: Two patients with unrecognized HPP were treated with anti-bone-breakdown drugs and did not show side effects from treatment.

source: Conclusions: Pathogenic variants in ALPL are common among patients with endocrinological diagnoses and low ALP.
target: Disease-causing mutations in ALPL are common in patients with hormonal diagnoses and low ALP.

source: Regarding diagnostics, genetic testing is necessary to identify mild HPP due to fluctuating biochemical findings.
target: Regarding detection, gene testing is needed to identify mild HPP due to diverse biochemical findings.

source: Antiresorptive treatment is a frequent reason for hypophosphatasaemia and effects of these agents in adults with a variant in ALPL and osteoporosis remain unclear and require further studies.
target: Anti-bone-breakdown treatment is frequent reason for low ALP levels and treatment effects in adults with an ALPL mutation and bone breakdown remain unclear and need further studies.

source: Summary: Hypophosphatasia (HPP) is a rare and under-recognised genetic defect in bone mineralisation.
target: Hypophosphatasia (HPP) is a rare and under-recognized genetic defect in bone mineralization. Bone mineralization is when bone become calcified.

source: Patients presenting with fragility fractures may be mistakenly diagnosed as having osteoporosis and prescribed antiresorptive therapy, a treatment which may increase fracture risk.
target: Patients presenting with fragility fractures may be mistakenly diagnosed as having osteoporosis (brittle bones). These patients may be prescribed antiresorptive therapy, or drugs that block the breakdown of bone. These drugs may increase fracture risk.

source: Adult-onset HPPhypophosphatasia was identified in a 40-year-old woman who presented with bilateral atypical femoral fractures after 4 years of denosumab therapy.
target: Adult-onset HPP was identified in a 40-year-old woman. She had with femur fractures after 4 years of using denosumba, a drug to treat osteoporosis.

source: A low serum alkaline phosphatase (ALP) and increased serum vitamin B6 level signalled the diagnosis, which was later confirmed by identification of two recessive mutations of the ALPL gene.
target: A low serum (blood) alkaline phosphatase (ALP) and increased serum vitamin B6 level signaled the diagnosis. Her diagnosis of HPP was later confirmed by identification of two recessive gene mutations of the ALPL gene. ALP is an enzyme that helps bone strength. ALPL is the gene that encodes ALP.

source: The patient was treated with teriparatide given the unavailability of ALP enzyme-replacement therapy (asfotase alfa).
target: The patient was treated with teriparatide (an osteoporosis drug) due to the unavailability of ALP enzyme-replacement therapy.

source: Fracture healing occurred, but impaired mobility persisted.
target: The fractures healed. However, her mobility was impaired.

source: HPP predisposes to atypical femoral fracture (AFF) during antiresorptive therapy; hence, bisphosphonates and denosumab are contraindicated in this condition.
target: HPP makes someone more likely to experience femur fractures during antiresorptive therapy. Because of this, bisphosphonates (drugs that limit bone density) and denosumab should not be given to people with this condition.

source: Screening patients with fracture or 'osteoporosis' to identify a low ALP level is recommended.
target: Healthcare workings should screen patients with fracture or 'osteoporosis' to identify a low ALP level.

source: Learning points: Hypophosphatasia (HPP) is a rare and under-recognised cause of bone fragility produced by impaired matrix mineralisation that can be misdiagnosed as a fragility fracture due to age-related bone loss.
target: HPP is a rare and under-recognized cause of bone fragility. HPP is caused by impaired mineralization that can be misdiagnosed as a fragility fracture due to age-related bone loss.

source: Antiresorptive therapy is contraindicated in HPP.
target: Antiresorptive therapy is not recommended in HPP treatment.

source: Low serum alkaline phosphatase (ALP) provides a clue to the diagnosis.
target: Low serum alkaline phosphatase (ALP) can aid in diagnosis.

source: Elevated serum vitamin B6 (an ALP substrate) is indicative of HPP, while identification of a mutation in the ALPL gene is confirmatory.
target: Elevated serum vitamin B6 (a chemical that works with ALP) is suggestive of HPP. Identifying mutations within the ALPL gene can confirm HPP status.

source: Enzyme therapy with recombinant ALP (asfotase alfa) is currently prohibitively costly.
target: Therapy with a chemical ALP reacts with, known as asfotase alfa, is currently unmanageably expensive.

source: Treatment with anabolic bone agents such as teriparatide has been reported, but whether normally mineralized bone is formed requires further study.
target: Treatment with anabolic (repairing) bone agents has been reported. However, if this treatment helps normally mineralized bone form needs further study.

source: Hypophosphatasia (HPP) is the heritable dento-osseous disease caused by loss-of-function mutation(s) of the gene ALPL that encodes the tissue-nonspecific isoenzyme of alkaline phosphatase (TNSALP).
target: Hypophosphatasia (HPP) is the heritable, tooth and bone disease. HPP is caused by mutations of the gene ALPL. ALPL encodes the tissue-nonspecific isoenzyme of alkaline phosphatase (TNSALP). TNSALP is an enzyme that assists in breaking down phosphate groups. Phosphate groups are important in activating proteins.

source: TNSALP is a cell-surface homodimeric phosphomonoester phosphohydrolase expressed in healthy people especially in the skeleton, liver, kidneys, and developing teeth.
target: TNSALP is expressed in healthy people, especially in the skeleton, liver, kidneys, and developing teeth.

source: In HPP, diminished TNSALP activity leads to extracellular accumulation of its natural substrates including inorganic pyrophosphate (PPi), an inhibitor of mineralization, and pyridoxal 5'-phosphate (PLP), the principal circulating form of vitamin B6 (B6).
target: In HPP, decreased TNSALP activity leads to extracellular accumulation of its natural substrates, including pyridoxal 5'-phosphate (PLP). PLP is the principal circulating form of vitamin B6 (B6).

source: Autosomal dominant and autosomal recessive inheritance involving >450 usually missense defects scattered throughout ALPL largely explains the remarkably broad-ranging severity of this inborn-error-of-metabolism.
target: Different mutations of ALPL largely explains the remarkably broad-ranging severity of this genetic inborn-error-of-metabolism.

source: In 1985 when we identified elevated plasma PLP as a biochemical hallmark of HPP, all 14 investigated affected children and adults had markedly increased PLP levels.
target: In 1985, elevated (blood) plasma PLP was identified as hallmark of HPP. In the study, all 14 investigated affected children and adults had increased PLP levels.

source: However, pyridoxal (PL), the dephosphorylated form of PLP that enters cells to cofactor many enzymatic reactions, was not low but often inexplicably elevated.
target: However, pyridoxal (PL), an alternate form of PLP that enters cells to cofactor (help) many enzymatic reactions, was often unexplainably elevated.

source: Levels of pyridoxic acid (PA), the B6 degradation product quantified to assess B6 sufficiency, were unremarkable.
target: Levels of pyridoxic acid (PA), a compound used to determine B6 levels in the body, were unremarkable.

source: Canonical signs or symptoms of B6 deficiency or toxicity were absent.
target: Signs or symptoms of B6 deficiency or toxicity were absent.

source: B6-dependent seizures in infants with life-threatening HPP were later explained by their profound deficiency of TNSALP activity blocking PLP dephosphorylation to PL and diminishing gamma-aminobutyric acid synthesis in the brain.
target: B6-dependent seizures in infants with life-threatening HPP were later explained by their low TNSALP activity. Lack of TNSALP activity was blocking PLP dephosphorylation, removal of phosphate groups, to PL. This diminished formation of gamma-aminobutyric acid, a neurotransmitter, in the brain.

source: Now, there is speculation that altered B6 metabolism causes further clinical complications in HPP.
target: It is theorized that abnormal B6 metabolism causes further health complications in HPP.

source: Herein, we assessed the plasma PL and PA levels accompanying previously reported elevated plasma PLP concentrations in 150 children and adolescents with HPP.
target: This study assessed (measured) the blood PL and PA levels in patients with previously reported elevated plasma PLP concentrations. The evaluated patient pool consisted of 150 children and adolescents with HPP.

source: Their mean (SD) plasma PL level was nearly double the mean for our healthy pediatric controls: 66.7 (59.0) nM versus 37.1 (22.2) nM (P < 0.0001), respectively.
target: Their average plasma PL level was nearly double the average of healthy pediatric controls.

source: Their PA levels were broader than our pediatric control range, but their mean value was normal; 40.2 (25.1) nM versus 39.3 (9.9) nM (P = 0.7793), respectively.
target: Their PA levels were broader than our pediatric (child) control range with a normal average value.

source: In contrast, adults with HPP often had plasma PL and PA levels suggestive of dietary B6 insufficiency.
target: In contrast, adults with HPP often had plasma PL and PA levels that suggest a dietary B6 insufficiency.

source: We discuss why the B6 levels of our pediatric patients with HPP would not cause B6 toxicity or deficiency, whereas in affected adults dietary B6 insufficiency can develop.
target: This paper discusses why B6 levels of children with HPP would not cause B6 toxicity or deficiency, whereas in affected adults dietary B6 insufficiency can develop.

source: Background: Tissue-nonspecific alkaline phosphatase (TNSALP) encoded by the ALPL gene is of particular importance for bone mineralization.
target: Background: Tissue-nonspecific alkaline phosphatase (TNSALP) is an enzyme that assists in breaking down phosphate groups. Phosphate groups are important in activating proteins. TNSALP is encoded by a gene, known as ALPL, that is of particular importance for bone mineralization or when minerals deposit onto bone.

source: Mutation in the ALPL gene can lead to persistent low ALP activity resulting in the rare disease Hypophosphatasia (HPP) that is characterized by disturbed bone and dental mineralization.
target: Mutation, or a structural change, in the ALPL gene can cause persistent low alkaline phosphatase (ALP) activity. ALP is an enzyme that helps bone strength. Low ALP activity can result in the rare disease Hypophosphatasia (HPP). HPP is characterized by disturbed bone and dental or tooth mineralization.

source: While severe forms are extremely rare with an estimated prevalence of 1/100.000, recent studies suggest that moderate form caused by heterozygous mutations are much more frequent with an estimated prevalence of 1/508.
target: Severe forms of HPP are extremely rare. However, there is an estimated prevalence of 1/100,000 people. Recent studies suggest that moderate HPP is more common with an estimated prevalence of 1/508 people.

source: The purpose of this study was to estimate the prevalence of low AP levels in the population based on laboratory measurements.
target: The aim of this study was to estimate the prevalence of low ALP levels in the population based on laboratory measurements.

source: Methods: In this study, the prevalence of low AP activity and elevated pyridoxal-5-phosphate (PLP) levels was analyzed in 6.918.126 measurements from 2011 to 2016 at a single laboratory in northern Germany.
target: The prevalence of low AP activity and elevated pyridoxal-5-phosphate (PLP) levels was analyzed in 6,918,126 measurements from 2011 to 2016. PLP is a co-enzyme or helper in several body processes.

source: Only laboratory values of subjects older than 18 years of age were included.
target: Only laboratory values of subjects older than 18 years of age were included.

source: Only the first measurement was included, all repeated values were excluded.
target: Only the first measurement was included. All repeated values were excluded.

source: Results: In total, 8.46% of the measurements of a total of 6.918.126 values showed a value < 30 U/L. 0.59% of the subjects with an ALP activity below 30 U/L had an additional PLP measurement.
target: In total, 8.46% of the measurements showed a value < 30 U/L. 0.59% of the subjects with an ALP activity below 30 U/L had an additional PLP measurement.

source: Here, 6.09% showed elevated pyridoxal-5-phosphate (PLP) levels.
target: Of the participants, 6.09% showed elevated pyridoxal-5-phosphate (PLP) levels.

source: This suggest that 0.52% (1:194) of subjects show laboratory signs of HPP.
target: This suggest that 0.52% of subjects show signs of HPP.

source: Conclusion: These data support the genetic estimation that the prevalence of moderate forms of HPP may be significantly higher than expected.
target: These data support the estimation that the prevalence of moderate forms of HPP is higher than expected.

source: Based on these data, we recommend automatically measurement of PLP in the case of low ALP activity and a notification to the ordering physician that HPP should be included in the differential diagnosis and further exploration is recommended.
target: The authors recommend automatic measurement of PLP in cases of low ALP activity. Physicians should be notified that HPP should be included in the diagnosis and further exploration is recommended.

source: Edema is an accumulation of fluid in the interstitial space that occurs as the capillary filtration exceeds the limits of lymphatic drainage, producing noticeable clinical signs and symptoms.
target: Edema is a build up of fluid in the body's tissues and does not drain properly, producing noticeable clinical signs and symptoms.

source: The rapid development of generalized pitting edema associated with systemic disease requires timely diagnosis and management.
target: The rapid development of pitting edema, which is when pressing into the swollen area leaves a pit or indentation, associated with diseases that affects other parts of the body requires timely diagnosis and management.

source: The chronic accumulation of edema in one or both lower extremities often indicates venous insufficiency, especially in the presence of dependent edema and hemosiderin deposition.
target: The ongoing build up of edema in one or both lower legs often suggests veins in the lower limbs are not allowing enough blood to flow to the heart, especially in the presence of gravity-related swelling and build up of iron deposits under the skin that has a shade of brown pigment.

source: Skin care is crucial in preventing skin breakdown and venous ulcers.
target: Skin care is crucial in preventing skin breakdown and venous ulcers, open sores on the skin from abnormal vein function.

source: Eczematous (stasis) dermatitis can be managed with emollients and topical steroid creams.
target: Inflamed skin in the lower legs called eczematous (stasis) dermatitis is a kind of eczema (skin swelling) caused by poor circulation and can be helped with moisturizers and topical steroid creams.

source: Patients who have had deep venous thrombosis should wear compression stockings to prevent postthrombotic syndrome.
target: Patients who have had a condition where a blood clot forms in the vein called deep venous thrombosis should wear compression stockings.

source: If clinical suspicion for deep venous thrombosis remains high after negative results are noted on duplex ultrasonography, further investigation may include magnetic resonance venography to rule out pelvic or thigh proximal venous thrombosis or compression.
target: If a doctor believes a diagnosis for deep venous thrombosis is possible even after negative (no detection) results are noted on tests, further investigation may include imaging scans to rule out pelvic or thigh proximal venous thrombosis or compression.

source: Obstructive sleep apnea may cause bilateral leg edema even in the absence of pulmonary hypertension.
target: Occasional airflow blockage during sleep called obstructive sleep apnea may cause edema in both legs even in the absence of high blood pressure.

source: Brawny, nonpitting skin with edema characterizes lymphedema, which can present in one or both lower extremities.
target: Discolored, non-pitting (non-indentable) skin with edema is often a sign of lymphedema, fluid build-up in soft tissues when the lymph system is damaged or blocked, which can present in one or both lower limbs.

source: Possible secondary causes of lymphedema include tumor, trauma, previous pelvic surgery, inguinal lymphadenectomy, and previous radiation therapy.
target: Possible other causes of lymphedema include tumor, trauma, previous pelvic surgery, inguinal lymphadenectomy (lymph node dissection), and previous radiation therapy.

source: Use of pneumatic compression devices or compression stockings may be helpful in these cases.
target: Use of compression devices that are inflatable sleeves worn on the legs or compression stockings may be helpful in these cases.

source: Edema is an accumulation of fluid in the interstitial space that occurs as the capillary filtration exceeds the limits of lymphatic drainage, producing noticeable clinical signs and symptoms.
target: Edema is a buildup of fluid in the body that occurs as the filtration rate exceeds the drainage rate, producing noticeable symptoms.

source: The rapid development of generalized pitting edema associated with systemic disease requires timely diagnosis and management.
target: The rapid development of swelling linked with full-body disease needs timely identification and treatment.

source: The chronic accumulation of edema in one or both lower extremities often indicates venous insufficiency, especially in the presence of dependent edema and hemosiderin deposition.
target: The long-lasting buildup of fluid in one or both legs often indicates blood vessel impairment, especially in the presence of swelling in the limbs and iron buildup.

source: Skin care is crucial in preventing skin breakdown and venous ulcers.
target: Skin care is needed in preventing skin breakdown and vein-related open sores.

source: Eczematous (stasis) dermatitis can be managed with emollients and topical steroid creams.
target: Skin inflammation from swelling in the legs can be managed with specific moisturizers and steroid creams.

source: Patients who have had deep venous thrombosis should wear compression stockings to prevent postthrombotic syndrome.
target: Patients who have had deep venous thrombosis (deep vein blood clotting) should wear compression socks to prevent its symptoms like pain and swelling.

source: If clinical suspicion for deep venous thrombosis remains high after negative results are noted on duplex ultrasonography, further investigation may include magnetic resonance venography to rule out pelvic or thigh proximal venous thrombosis or compression.
target: If clinicians remain suspicious of deep venous thrombosis even when lab tests do not detect it, further lab tests may be performed to rule out other forms of blood clotting and compression.

source: Obstructive sleep apnea may cause bilateral leg edema even in the absence of pulmonary hypertension.
target: Obstructuve sleep apnea (disorder in which breathing is consistently interrupted during sleep by a blockage) may cause swelling in both legs even without lung-related high blood pressure.

source: Brawny, nonpitting skin with edema characterizes lymphedema, which can present in one or both lower extremities.
target: Sturdy skin with swelling characterizes lymphedema (fluid buildup when the body's drainage system is blocked), which can present in one or both legs.

source: Possible secondary causes of lymphedema include tumor, trauma, previous pelvic surgery, inguinal lymphadenectomy, and previous radiation therapy.
target: Possible secondary causes of lymphedema include tumor, blunt force, previous hip surgery, lymph node (immune system organ) removal, and previous radiation therapy.

source: Aim: Leg edema, observed on comprehensive geriatric assessment (CGA) of 142 elderly outpatients with a variety of chronic diseases, was studied clinically to clarify its incidence and its associated risk factors.
target: In this study, leg edema (fluid swelling) is studied to understand how frequently it occurs and its associated risk factors. Leg edema is observed on a detailed evaluation of older and frail people called the comprehensive geriatric (elderly) assessment of 142 elderly outpatients with a variety of chronic diseases.

source: Methods: The severity of pitting edema was assessed at 3 points, namely, the pretibial edge, medial malleolus, and the dorsum of the foot.
target: The severity of pitting edema, a condition where excess fluid builds up in one part of the body and pressing into the swollen area leaves a pit or indentation, is assessed at 3 places: the shins, the bony bump on the inside of the ankle, and the top part of the foot that faces up when standing.

source: On palpation, edema was graded as 0 to 3 for each point on one leg, the sum of which was used as the edema score.
target: When pressing on the swollen area, edema is graded as 0 to 3 for each point on one leg, the sum of which was used as the edema score.

source: According to the edema score, subjects were divided into 3 groups; the moderate to severe (MS) group, the slight to mild (SM) group, and the group without pitting edema.
target: According to the edema score, patients are divided into 3 groups; the moderate to severe group, the slight to mild group, and the group without pitting edema.

source: The MS group was defined as having an edema score of 4 or more or edema of grade 2 or more, while the SM group was defined as having an edema score of 2 to 3 points without edema of grade 2 or more.
target: The moderate to severe group is defined as having an edema score of 4 or more or edema of grade 2 or more, while the slight to mild group is defined as having an edema score of 2 to 3 points without edema of grade 2 or more.

source: The status of underlying disease, vascular risks, varicose veins, medications, daily activity, nutrition, total protein (TP), albumin, brain natriuretic peptide (BNP), and the estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) were compared among the 3 groups.
target: The following tests and measurements are compared among the 3 groups: the status of underlying disease, risks to developing heart problems, varicose (twisted, enlarged) veins, medications, daily activity, nutrition, tests for proteins in the body, and tests for how well the kidneys are working.

source: Results: There were 36 subjects in the MS group and 19 subjects in the SM group.
target: There are 36 patients in the moderate to severe group and 19 subjects in the slight to mild group.

source: Diabetes, atrial fibrillation, varicose veins, and polypharmacy were more frequent in the MS group than in the control group.
target: Diabetes, an irregular heart beat (atrial fibrillation), varicose veins, and being on multiple drugs at one time are more frequent in the moderate to severe group than in the comparison group.

source: Sedentary life style, house-bound, and gait trouble were significantly more frequent in the MS and SM groups.
target: Sedentary life style (no or little physical activity), house-bound, and walking trouble are much more frequent in the moderate to severe and slight to mild groups.

source: There were no significant differences in the scores of the Mini-Nutritional Assessment Short Form among the groups, although both the body weight and calf circumference in the MS group were significantly greater than those in the group without pitting edema.
target: There are no significant differences in the scores on malnourishment (poor nutrition) among the groups, although both the body weight and calf circumference (size) in the moderate to severe group are much greater than those in the group without pitting edema.

source: Low serum TP, albumin and eGFR were seen in the MS group as well as high BNP levels.
target: Low levels of total protein in the blood, another protein made by liver called albumin, and kidney function tests are seen in the moderate to severe group as well as high levels of a protein made by heart and blood vessels called BNP.

source: Multiple regression analysis revealed diabetes, varicose veins, sedentarism, and hypoalbuminemia as risk factors associated with leg edema (R(2)=0.365, p<0.0001).
target: Data analysis revealed diabetes, varicose veins, sedentarism (inactivity), and low levels of the protein albumin as risk factors associated with leg edema.

source: Conclusion: Leg edema was frequent in the elderly outpatients and was associated strongly with diabetes, varicose veins, sedentarism, and hypoalbuminemia.
target: In conclusion, leg edema is frequent in the elderly outpatients and is associated strongly with diabetes, varicose veins, sedentarism, and low levels of albumin in the blood.

source: These findings suggest that advising against a sedentary life style could help the resolution of edema, and also indicates the clinical usefulness of CGA.
target: These findings suggest that advising against an inactive life style could help resolve edema, and also indicates the usefulness of comprehensive geriatric assessment to evaluate older populations.

source: Furthermore, leg edema should be seriously considered along with nutritional assessment because edema could influence various anthropometric parameters.
target: Furthermore, leg edema should be seriously considered along with evaluating the diet of patients because edema could influence various physical measurements of the body.

source: Symmetrical leg swelling formed in the course of the years is a common and in most cases benign phenomenon that is mostly encountered in the aged population, especially in women.
target: Leg swelling in both legs over time is common, and in most cases unharmful, and is mostly found in the older population, especially in women.

source: Venous insufficiency of the lower limbs is the most common cause of symmetrical leg swelling among those over 50 years of age.
target: Veins in the lower limbs that do not allow enough blood to flow to the heart, called venous insufficiency, is the most common cause of swelling in legs among those over the age of 50.

source: Diseases of the essential organs such as the heart, the liver and the kidneys are excluded during the initial stage.
target: Diseases of the important organs such as the heart, the liver and the kidneys are excluded during the first stage.

source: Pitting edema occurs both in venous insufficiency and in right-sided heart failure.
target: Pitting edema, a condition where excess fluid builds up in one part of the body and pressing into the swollen area leaves a pit or indentation, occurs both in venous insufficiency and in right-sided heart failure.

source: Basic tests and drug history are usually sufficient to exclude a host of general causes of the edema.
target: Basic tests and drug history are usually enough to exclude a number of general causes of the edema.

source: Rheumatologists are increasingly aware of the entity synovitis with pitting edema.
target: Specialists who treat diseases in the joints, muscles, and bones, called rheumatologists, are increasingly aware of swollen joints (synovitis) with pitting edema, a condition where excess fluid builds up in one part of the body and pressing into the swollen area leaves a pit or indentation.

source: The remitting seronegative symmetrical synovitis with pitting edema (RS3PE) syndrome has been reported with an array of conditions that include polymyalgia rheumatica, rheumatoid arthritis, Sjögren's syndrome and psoriatic arthropathy.
target: The remitting seronegative symmetrical synovitis with pitting edema (RS3PE) syndrome is a rare disease with swollen joints and pitting edema. RS3PE has been reported with a number of conditions that include polymyalgia rheumatica (a disorder that causes muscle pain and stiffness, mainly in the shoulders and hips), rheumatoid arthritis (arthritis whereby immune cells attack healthy cells), an immune system disorder that causes dry eyes and mouth called Sjögren's syndrome, and a type of arthritis linked to psoriasis (a skin disease causing red, itchy scaly patches).

source: Synovitis with pitting edema is now being increasingly recognized with systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE).
target: Synovitis with pitting edema is now being increasingly seen in patients with systemic lupus erythematosus (lupus), a chronic disease where the body's immune system mistakenly attacks healthy cells and tissues.

source: We report a patient who presented with edema of hands and feet and was diagnosed eventually with definite SLE.
target: Researchers report a patient who presented with edema (swelling) of hands and feet and was diagnosed eventually with definite lupus.

source: With magnetic resonance imaging, joint effusions and tenosynovitis were confirmed to be associated with the otherwise-unexplained extremity edema.
target: With imaging tools, fluid build up in the joints and inflammation (redness and swelling from fighting an infection) of the top layer of tendons (tenosynovitis) were confirmed to be associated with the otherwise-unexplained edema in the limbs.

source: 46 year old male with past medical history of schizoaffective disorder and chronic lower back pain, was admitted for management of worsening depression and anxiety.
target: A 46 year old male with past medical history of schizoaffective (reality-distorting) disorder, a mental disorder, and chronic lower back pain, is admitted to a medical facility to manage worsening depression and anxiety.

source: He was started on gabapentin, 300mg twice daily for his back pain and anxiety symptoms.
target: He is started on a medicine called gabapentin (nerve pain medication) for his back pain and anxiety symptoms.

source: His only other medication was hydrocodone.
target: His only other medication is an opioid pain reliever called hydrocodone.

source: Over next few days, he started developing worsening bilateral lower extremity edema.
target: Over the next few days, he starts developing worsening edema, swelling caused by too much fluid trapped in the body's tissues, in both legs.

source: He did not have any cardiovascular related symptoms.
target: He does not have any cardiovascular (heart) related symptoms.

source: Physical exam was only significant for 3+ pitting edema with all laboratory values and imaging being unremarkable.
target: A physical exam found pitting edema, where pressing into the swollen area leaves a pit or indentation. All other lab tests and imaging scans did not show anything significant.

source: Gabapentin was discontinued and his lower extremity swelling improved over subsequent days.
target: Gabapentin is stopped and the swelling in his legs improved over the following days.

source: Incidence of pedal edema with gabapentin use is approximately 7 to 7.5% with all studies being in elderly patients receiving doses above 1200 mg/day.
target: In other studies, the occurrence of swollen feet (pedal edema) while on gabapentin is about 7 to 7.5% in elderly patients receiving doses above 1200 mg/day.

source: This case illustrates that lower doses of gabapentin can also cause this adverse effect.
target: This case illustrates that lower doses of gabapentin can also cause this negative effect.

source: It is important to recognize this adverse effect because gabapentin is used in conditions like diabetic neuropathy, which is associated with multiple co-morbidities that can give rise to bilateral leg swelling.
target: It is important to recognize this negative effect because gabapentin is used in conditions like nerve damage common with diabetes (diabetic neuropathy), which is associated with multiple illnesses that can give rise to swelling in both legs.

source: 46 year old male with past medical history of schizoaffective disorder and chronic lower back pain, was admitted for management of worsening depression and anxiety.
target: We admitted a 46 year old male to treat his worsening depression and anxiety. He had a prior history of symptoms of schizophrenia and mood disorders and long-lasting lower back pain.

source: He was started on gabapentin, 300mg twice daily for his back pain and anxiety symptoms.
target: He was started on gabapentin (nerve pain medication), 300 mg twice daily for his back pain and anxiety symptoms.

source: His only other medication was hydrocodone.
target: His only other medication was hydrocodone, a pain and cough medication.

source: Over next few days, he started developing worsening bilateral lower extremity edema.
target: Over the next few days, he developed worsening swelling in both legs.

source: He did not have any cardiovascular related symptoms.
target: He did not have any heart-related symptoms.

source: Physical exam was only significant for 3+ pitting edema with all laboratory values and imaging being unremarkable.
target: Physical exam only noted serious swelling with all lab values and imaging not being serious.

source: Gabapentin was discontinued and his lower extremity swelling improved over subsequent days.
target: Gabapentin use was stopped, and his leg swelling improved over the next few days.

source: Incidence of pedal edema with gabapentin use is approximately 7 to 7.5% with all studies being in elderly patients receiving doses above 1200 mg/day.
target: Frequency of leg swelling with gabapentin use is around 7 to 7.5% with all studies being in elderly patients receiving doses above 1200 mg/day.

source: This case illustrates that lower doses of gabapentin can also cause this adverse effect.
target: This case shows that lower doses of gabapentin can also cause this harmful effect.

source: It is important to recognize this adverse effect because gabapentin is used in conditions like diabetic neuropathy, which is associated with multiple co-morbidities that can give rise to bilateral leg swelling.
target: This serious effect should be noted since gabapentin is used in conditions like diabetic nerve pain, which is linked with other diseases that can give rise to swelling in both legs.

source: Presence of gabapentin induced leg swelling can thus confound the clinical picture.
target: Presence of gabapentin-caused leg swelling can thus confuse the diagnosis.

source: Background: Leg edema is a common adverse effect of dihydropyridine Calcium Channel Blockers (CCB) that may need dose reduction or drug withdrawal, adversely affecting the antihypertensive efficacy.
target: Leg edema is a common side effect of dihydropyridine Calcium Channel Blockers (CCB), medicines taken to control blood pressure and other conditions. The dose amount of CBB drugs may need to be reduced or stopped, negatively impacting the drugs' use to control blood pressure.

source: Leg edema is reported to occur less often with (S)-amlodipine compared to conventional racemic amlodipine.
target: Leg edema (fluid swelling) is reported to occur less often with a type of CBB medicine called (S)-amlodipine compared to the regular racemic amlodipine medicine.

source: We aimed to find the incidence of leg edema as a primary outcome and antihypertensive efficacy with (S)-amlodipine compared to conventional amlodipine.
target: Researchers aim to find the number of times leg edema appeared and how well blood pressure is lowered with (S)-amlodipine compared to conventional amlodipine.

source: Methods: This prospective, double-blind, controlled clinical trial randomized 172 hypertensive patients, not controlled on beta-blockers (BB) and angiotensin converting enzyme inhibitors/angiotensin receptor blockers (ACEI/ARB), to either conventional amlodipine (5-10 mg; n = 86) or (S)-amlodipine (2.5-5 mg; n = 86), while continuing their previous anti-hypertensive medications.
target: This clinical trial followed 172 patients with high blood pressure, not controlled by using beta-blockers (BB) drugs or angiotensin converting enzyme inhibitors/angiotensin receptor blockers (ACEI/ARB) that keep blood vessels open. These patients are randomly placed in groups to receive either standard amlodipine (86 patients) or (S)-amlodipine (86 patients), while continuing their previous anti-hypertensive medications.

source: Sample was sufficient to find a difference in edema between the interventions with 80 % power at 5 % significance level.
target: The number of patients in this study is sufficient to find a difference in edema between the two groups.

source: Intension to treat analysis (ITT) for safety data and per protocol analysis for efficacy data was performed.
target: Data from both treatment groups are analyzed during the study.

source: Fischer's exact test was applied to observe difference between responder rates and proportions of subjects having peripheral edema in the two groups.
target: Data are analyzed to observe the difference between the two groups on how they responded to the treatment and the number of people who develop peripheral edema (swelling in lower legs and hands).

source: Pitting edema test scores were compared using Mann-Whitney test.
target: Pitting edema, a condition where excess fluid builds up in one part of the body and pressing into the swollen area leaves a pit or indentation, is measured and scored.

source: Results: Altogether 146 patients (amlodipine, n = 76 and (S)-amlodipine, n = 70) completed 120 days treatment.
target: Altogether 146 patients completed 120 days treatment: 76 patients in the standard amlodipine group and 70 in the (S)-amlodipine group.

source: Demographic variables and treatment adherence were comparable in the two groups.
target: Characteristics such as age and sex and how well patients took the medicine as directed are compared between the two groups.

source: Incidence of new edema after randomization was 31.40 % in test group and 46.51 % in control group [p = 0.03; absolute risk reduction (ARR) = 15.1 %; Number Needed to Treat (NNT) = 7, ITT analysis].
target: New edema is 31.40 % in the (S)-amlodipine group and 46.51 % in the standard group.

source: Pitting edema score and patient rated edema score increased significantly in the control compared to test group (p = 0.038 and 0.036 respectively) after treatment period.
target: Pitting edema score and patient rated edema score increase significantly in the amlodipine compared to (S)-amlodipine group after the treatment period.

source: Edema scores increased significantly in the control group from baseline (p < 0.0001).
target: Edema scores increase significantly in the amlodipine group from the start of the study.

source: Responders in blood pressure were 98.57 % in test and 98.68 % in control group.
target: Responders in blood pressure are 98.57 % in (S)-amlodipine and 98.68 % in amlodipine group.

source: Most common adverse events (AE) were pitting edema and increased urinary frequency.
target: Most common side effects are pitting edema and increase in urinary frequency.

source: Incidence of all AEs other than edema was similar in both groups.
target: Number of all side effects other than edema is similar in both groups.

source: Two serious AEs occurred unrelated to therapy.
target: Two serious side effects occur that are unrelated to therapy.

source: Biochemical and ECG parameters in the two groups were comparable.
target: Results from other blood and heart tests in the two groups are similar.

source: Conclusions: In hypertensive patients not controlled on prior BB and ACEI/ARB therapy, addition of (S)-amlodipine besylate at half the dose of conventional amlodipine provides better tolerability with reduced incidence of peripheral edema, and equal antihypertensive efficacy compared to amlodipine given at usual doses.
target: In patients with high blood pressure not controlled on prior BB and ACEI/ARB therapy, adding (S)-amlodipine besylate at half the dose of standard amlodipine provides few side effects. There is also a reduced number of peripheral edema, and equal ability to lower blood pressure compared to amlodipine given at usual doses.

source: We encountered an elderly male patient who after cardiac surgery for mitral stenosis had refractory pitting edema in both legs involving painful leg joints after a 1-month history of waxing and waning arthralgia.
target: Doctors saw an elderly male patient who after heart surgery had pitting edema in both legs, a condition where excess fluid builds up in part of the body and pressing into the swollen area leaves a pit or indentation. The man also had painful leg joints after a 1-month history of periodic changes in joint stiffness.

source: His family doctor had prescribed a combination of diuretics, 40 mg furosemide and 25 mg spironolactone; however, pitting edema in his lower legs persisted.
target: His family doctor had prescribed a combination of diuretics, medicines that help remove salt and water from the body; however, pitting edema in his lower legs continued.

source: He was diagnosed with worsening of congestive heart failure because of a previous cardiac surgery and was transferred to our hospital.
target: He was diagnosed with worsening of congestive heart failure (inefficient heart pumping) because of a previous heart surgery and transferred to the hospital.

source: On admission, we closely observed the patient's condition and noticed that his body temperature increased to 38.0 °C every evening.
target: On admission to the hospital, doctors closely observed the patient's condition and noticed that his body temperature increased to 100.4 F (38.0 C) every evening.

source: Furthermore, his ankle joints felt feverish and were swollen.
target: Additionally, his ankle joints felt feverish and were swollen.

source: Therefore, we suspected polyarthritis as an etiology, although we initially suspected rheumatoid arthritis (RA).
target: Therefore, doctors suspected arthritis in at least five joints (polyarthritis) as a potential cause, although doctors first thought it was rheumatoid arthritis (RA - a long-lasting arthritis from immune cells attacking healthy cells).

source: Antibody testing did not support RA diagnosis; therefore we concluded the association of remitting seronegative symmetrical synovitis with pitting edema (RS3PE) syndrome with his condition.
target: Testing did not support a rheumatoid arthritis diagnosis; therefore, doctors concluded the association of remitting seronegative symmetrical synovitis with pitting edema (RS3PE) syndrome with his condition. RS3PE is a rare disease with swollen joints and pitting edema.

source: After daily treatment with 15 mg prednisolone, the refractory edema symptom dramatically improved.
target: After daily treatment with a steroid medication called prednisolone, the edema symptom greatly improved.

source: The concept of RS3PE syndrome could explain such as an impressive clinical course.
target: The concept of RS3PE syndrome could explain such as an impressive clinical result.

source: <Learning objective: Physicians encounter patients with pitting edema of unknown etiology in daily clinical practice.
target: Doctors encounter patients with pitting edema of unknown cause in daily clinical practice.

source: In particular, cardiologists usually tend to prescribe diuretics for patients with pitting edema in their legs.
target: In particular, heart specialists (cardiologists) usually tend to prescribe diuretics for patients with pitting edema in their legs.

source: Cardiologists should consider RS3PE syndrome as a differential diagnosis, for patients with localized pitting edema in their extremities.
target: Cardiologists should consider RS3PE syndrome as a differential (potential) diagnosis, for patients with pitting edema in one location in their limbs.

source: This report cautions regarding arbitrarily prescribing diuretics for localized pitting edema.>.
target: This report cautions prescribing diuretics for localized pitting edema.

source: May-Thurner syndrome (MTS) is a clinical condition characterized by the compression of the left iliac vein by the right iliac artery.
target: May-Thurner syndrome (MTS) is a clinical condition where the left iliac vein located in the abdomen that goes into the left leg is compressed by the right iliac artery which is a vein that goes into the right leg.

source: This condition predisposes the patient to deep venous thrombosis (DVT).
target: Having this condition makes it easier for the patient to develop deep venous thrombosis (DVT), a serious condition where a blood clot forms in the vein.

source: We present the case of a 30-year﻿-old female who arrived at the emergency department of our facility with progressive left leg swelling for four weeks, with low-risk probability for DVT.
target: Researchers present the case of a 30-year﻿-old female who arrived at the emergency room with gradual left leg swelling for four weeks, with low-risk for developing DVT.

source: Examination revealed left leg swelling with pitting edema extending up to the knee.
target: An exam revealed left leg swelling with pitting edema up to the knee, a condition where excess fluid builds up in one part of the body and pressing into the swollen area leaves a pit or indentation.

source: Her calf muscle was tender to palpation.
target: Her calf muscle was tender to light touch.

source: Dorsalis pedis, anterior tibial, and posterior tibial pulsations were fairly palpable due to the edema; however, the rest of her pulsations were appropriately felt.
target: Veins in the foot, in the leg called the anterior tibial, and in the lower leg can be fairly felt due to the edema; however, the rest of her pulses were appropriately felt.

source: Therefore, the provisional diagnosis of possible DVT was made, and further investigations were requested.
target: Therefore, the early diagnosis of possible DVT was made, and further investigations were requested.

source: We present this case intending to highlight the clinical presentation of May-Thurner syndrome, its diagnosis, and treatment.
target: Researchers present this case intending to highlight the clinical signs and symptoms of May-Thurner syndrome, its diagnosis, and treatment.

source: May-Thurner syndrome (MTS) is a clinical condition characterized by the compression of the left iliac vein by the right iliac artery.
target: May-Thurner syndrome (MTS) is a condition in which the main right leg blood vessel compresses the main left leg blood vessel.

source: This condition predisposes the patient to deep venous thrombosis (DVT).
target: MTS puts the patient at risk of deep venous thrombosis (DVT), or deep blood clotting.

source: We present the case of a 30-year﻿-old female who arrived at the emergency department of our facility with progressive left leg swelling for four weeks, with low-risk probability for DVT.
target: We describe a 30-year-old female who arrived at the emergency department with worsening left leg swelling for four weeks, with low-risk chance for DVT.

source: Examination revealed left leg swelling with pitting edema extending up to the knee.
target: Exams showed left leg swelling with serious swelling up to the knee.

source: Her calf muscle was tender to palpation.
target: Her lower leg calf muscle was tender to touch.

source: Dorsalis pedis, anterior tibial, and posterior tibial pulsations were fairly palpable due to the edema; however, the rest of her pulsations were appropriately felt.
target: Various blood vessel pulsations in the leg were noticeable due to the swelling. However, the rest of her pulsations were examined by touch.

source: Therefore, the provisional diagnosis of possible DVT was made, and further investigations were requested.
target: Therefore, the identification of possibly DVT was made. Further invesetigations were requested.

source: We present this case intending to highlight the clinical presentation of May-Thurner syndrome, its diagnosis, and treatment.
target: We present this case to highlight the symptoms of May-Thurner syndrome, its identification, and treatment.

source: BACKGROUND In lymphedema, an imbalance in the formation and absorption of lymph causes accumulation of protein-rich fluid in the interstitium of the most gravity-dependent parts of the body.
target: In lymphedema, an imbalance of a fluid called lymph causes a build up of protein-rich fluid, usually in the arms and legs.

source: Diagnosis is usually made based on patient medical history and a physical examination showing a typical appearance of the affected body part.
target: Diagnosis is usually made based on patient medical history and a physical examination of the affected body part.

source: Differential diagnosis is confirmed by imaging.
target: The diagnosis may be confirmed by imaging, such as x-rays or another scan.

source: CASE REPORT Primary lymphedema is inherited in through an autosomal dominant pattern.
target: Primary lymphedema, also called hereditary lymphedema, is inherited (passed down from parent to child) through genes.

source: Congestive cardiac failure and non-filarial infections predispose patients to the secondary form of lymphedema, elephantiasis nostras verrucosa (ENV).
target: Congestive heart failure (inefficient heart pumping) and infections from soil particles make it easier for patients to develop the secondary form of lymphedema, elephantiasis nostras verrucosa (ENV). ENV is a rare chronic condition caused by ongoing infections with bacteria and causes a part of the body, usually the lower part of the body, to be extremely enlarged.

source: We present the case of a 65-year-old man with lymphedema praecox complicated by congestive cardiac failure.
target: Researchers describe the case of a 65-year-old man with lymphedema complicated by congestive heart failure.

source: The patient was experiencing worsening left leg swelling and had a prior history of unilateral leg swelling at puberty.
target: The patient was experiencing worsening left leg swelling and had a prior history of leg swelling at puberty (when a child's body changes to become an adult) due to disease.

source: The condition was inherited through an autosomal dominant pattern, as his father, elder brother, and nephew were diagnosed with the same disease.
target: The condition was inherited, as his father, elder brother, and nephew were diagnosed with the same disease.

source: The left leg showed non-pitting edema with indurated, woody skin and lichenification.
target: The left leg showed non-pitting edema (there was no indentation when the area of swelling is pressed) with woody skin due to scaring that was also thick and leathery.

source: The right leg had mild pitting edema.
target: The right leg had mild pitting edema.

source: There were numerous verrucous folds and cobblestone-like nodules, and plaques and a painless ulcer on the left leg.
target: There were numerous folds and cobblestone-like lumps, raised red patches of the skin, and a painless ulcer (sore) on the left leg.

source: Laboratory evaluation demonstrated an elevated B-type natriuretic peptide.
target: Lab tests showed high levels of a hormone made by the heart called B-type natriuretic peptide.

source: He was treated with compression stockings and inelastic multi-layer bandaging and was administered limb decongestive treatment.
target: He was treated with compression stockings and bandaging and given decongestive treatment in the limb which includes several techniques such as draining fluid.

source: After 1 week of therapy, his swelling had somewhat improved.
target: After 1 week of therapy, his swelling had somewhat improved.

source: CONCLUSIONS Various conditions can cause ENV and it can superimpose on any form of hereditary lymphedema.
target: In conclusion, various conditions can cause elephantiasis nostras verrucosa (ENV), and it can be an additional condition on any form of hereditary lymphedema.

source: The most effective strategy for this condition seems to be a thorough workup of the underlying cause of the ENV and early intervention.
target: The most effective strategy for this condition seems to be a detailed workup of the underlying cause of the ENV and early treatment and management.

source: BACKGROUND Lipedema is a common condition that presents as excessive fat deposition in the extremities, initially sparing the trunk, ankles, and feet, and is found mainly in women, usually occurring after puberty or pregnancy.
target: Lipedema is a common condition where there is too much fat in the arms and legs and is found mainly in women, usually occurring after puberty (when a child's body changes to become an adult) or pregnancy.

source: Lipedema can progress to include lipo-lymphedema of the ankles and feet.
target: Lipedema can progress to include lipo-lymphedema, which is a build up of fluid in addition to excess fat, in the ankles and feet.

source: This report is of a 41-year old woman with Stage 3 lipedema and lipo-lymphedema with excessive fat of the lower body since puberty, with progression to swollen ankles and feet despite dietary caloric restriction.
target: This report is of a 41-year old woman with Stage 3 lipedema and lipo-lymphedema with excessive fat of the lower body since puberty. The woman's condition has advanced to swollen ankles and feet despite being on a low calorie diet.

source: CASE REPORT A 41-year-old woman noticed increased fat in her legs since age 12.
target: The 41-year-old woman noticed increased fat in her legs since age 12.

source: Her weight and leg size increased until age 21, when she reached a maximum weight of 165 kg, and underwent a Roux-En-Y gastric bypass.
target: Her weight and leg size increased until age 21, when she reached a maximum weight of 364 pounds (165 kg), and had a procedure called gastric bypass that involves a doctor placing a small pouch in the stomach and food only goes into that pouch.

source: Over 12 months, she lost 74.8 kg.
target: Over 12 months, she lost 165 pounds (74.8 kg).

source: Her trunk significantly reduced in weight, but her legs did not.
target: Her trunk (chest, stomach, and back) significantly reduced in weight, but her legs did not.

source: Fifteen years later, during recovery from hysterectomy surgery, she became progressively weaker and swollen over her entire body.
target: Fifteen years later, during recovery from surgery, she became continuously weaker and swollen over her entire body.

source: Laboratory test results showed hypoalbuminemia (2.0 g/dL), lymphopenia, and hypolipoproteinemia.
target: Lab test results showed hypoalbuminemia (low levels of the albumin protein in the blood), making it harder to move substances in the body. Tests also show low levels of white blood cells and low levels of fat lipids in the blood.

source: She was diagnosed with protein and calorie malnutrition with marked gut edema requiring prolonged parenteral nutrition.
target: She was not eating enough protein and energy (through calories) to meet nutritional needs with edema (swelling) in the stomach, requiring that more nutrition be given through the vein.

source: After restoration of normal protein, her health returned and her pitting edema resolved, but her extremities remained enlarged.
target: After restoring protein to normal levels, her health returned and her pitting edema due to excess fluid resolved, but her arms and legs remained enlarged.

source: She was subsequently diagnosed with lipedema.
target: She was later diagnosed with lipedema.

source: CONCLUSIONS This report demonstrates that early and correct diagnosis of lipedema is important, as women who believe the condition is due to obesity may suffer the consequences of calorie or protein-calorie deficiency in an attempt to lose weight.
target: In conclusion, this report shows that early and correct diagnosis of lipedema is important. Women who believe the condition is due to obesity may suffer from not enough calorie or protein-calories in an attempt to lose weight.

source: Sarcoidosis is characterized by granuloma formation, the macrophage being the most important building block.
target: Sarcoidosis is a disease that is characterized by granuloma formation or the tiny growth collections of inflammation (infection-fighting) cells. One of these cell types is the immune cell, macrophage. Macrophages are one of the most important building blocks of this disease.

source: The activated macrophage in sarcoidosis produces interleukin-1 (II-1).
target: Macrophage in sarcoidosis produces interleukin-1 (II-1), a protein that helps fight infections.

source: It is well known that interleukin-1, among other functions, stimulates the hepatic production of C-reactive protein.
target: Interleukin-1 has many functions, including the stimulation of the hepatic (liver) production of C-reactive protein. C-reactive protein is a type of protein found in the blood that increases during times of internal inflammation (redness and swelling from infection-fighting).

source: We therefore prospectively measured the serum C-reactive protein in 17 patients with active pulmonary sarcoidosis, 10 patients with other chronic interstitial lung diseases of unknown etiology, 11 patients with active lung tuberculosis, and 10 healthy volunteers.
target: Serum (blood) C-reactive protein was measured in 48 patients. Seventeen of the patients had active sarcoidosis in the lung. Ten patients had other lung diseases of unknown origin. Eleven patients had active lung tuberculosis (a serious lung infection). Ten patients were healthy volunteers.

source: Serum C-reactive protein was assayed by enzymoimmunodiffusion test.
target: Serum C-reactive protein was measured.

source: The serum C-reactive protein was negative in 13 patients suffering from active sarcoidosis and positive in four.
target: The serum C-reactive protein was negative (no detection) in 13 patients suffering from active sarcoidosis and positive in four.

source: Patients with other interstitial lung diseases had negative results in 7 and positive in 3 cases.
target: Patients with other lung diseases had negative results in 7 cases and positive (was detected) in 3 cases.

source: The analyses of C-reactive protein in patients with sputum positive lung tuberculosis were positive in 10 cases.
target: The analyses of C-reactive protein in patients with lung tuberculosis were positive in 10 cases.

source: All the healthy controls had negative C-reactive protein measurements.
target: All the healthy controls had negative C-reactive protein measurements.

source: The difference between the groups was statistically significant when sarcoidosis and tuberculosis serum C-reactive protein measurements were compared (p less than 0.01), as well as the difference between the group of other interstitial lung diseases and tuberculosis (p less than 0.01).
target: The difference between the groups was statistically significant when sarcoidosis and tuberculosis serum C-reactive protein measurements were compared. There was also a significant difference between the group of other lung diseases and tuberculosis.

source: In this respect, the measurements of serum C-reactive protein are valuable in the differentiation of sarcoidosis and other chronic interstitial lung diseases of unknown etiology from tuberculosis and other diseases which are known to induce an acute phase response.
target: Levels of serum C-reactive protein are valuable in the identification of sarcoidosis and other lung diseases of unknown origin from tuberculosis and other diseases.

source: Sarcoidosis is characterized by granuloma formation, the macrophage being the most important building block.
target: Sarcoidosis is a disease characterized by growths of inflammatory cells in the body.

source: The activated macrophage in sarcoidosis produces interleukin-1 (II-1).
target: The activated macrophage, a specialized white blood cell defender against invaders, in sarcoidosis produces interleukin-1 (Il-1), a molecule which signals inflammation.

source: It is well known that interleukin-1, among other functions, stimulates the hepatic production of C-reactive protein.
target: Interleukin-1, among other functions, activates the liver production of C-reactive protein, a marker of inflammation.

source: We therefore prospectively measured the serum C-reactive protein in 17 patients with active pulmonary sarcoidosis, 10 patients with other chronic interstitial lung diseases of unknown etiology, 11 patients with active lung tuberculosis, and 10 healthy volunteers.
target: We measured the blood C-reactive protein in 17 patients with active lung-related sarcoidosis, 10 with other long-lasting lung diseases of unknown cause, 11 with active lung tuberculosis (a bacterial lung infection), and 10 healthy volunteers.

source: Serum C-reactive protein was assayed by enzymoimmunodiffusion test.
target: Blood C-reactive protein was measured with a specific lab test.

source: The serum C-reactive protein was negative in 13 patients suffering from active sarcoidosis and positive in four.
target: The blood C-reactive protein was not detected in 13 patients with active sacroidosis and was detected in four.

source: Patients with other interstitial lung diseases had negative results in 7 and positive in 3 cases.
target: C-reactive protein was not detected in 7 patients with other lung diseases and was detected in 3 cases.

source: The analyses of C-reactive protein in patients with sputum positive lung tuberculosis were positive in 10 cases.
target: The tests of C-reactive protein in patients with lung tuberculosis-related mucus was detected in 10 cases.

source: All the healthy controls had negative C-reactive protein measurements.
target: No C-reactive protein was detected in healthy patients.

source: The difference between the groups was statistically significant when sarcoidosis and tuberculosis serum C-reactive protein measurements were compared (p less than 0.01), as well as the difference between the group of other interstitial lung diseases and tuberculosis (p less than 0.01).
target: There was a difference between groups when sarcoidosis and tuberculosis blood C-reactive protein measurements were compared, along with a difference between the group of other lung diseases and tuberculosis.

source: In this respect, the measurements of serum C-reactive protein are valuable in the differentiation of sarcoidosis and other chronic interstitial lung diseases of unknown etiology from tuberculosis and other diseases which are known to induce an acute phase response.
target: Measuring blood C-reactive protein is valuable to differentiate sacroidosis and other lung-lasting lung diseases of unknown causes from tuberculosis and other diseases known to cause an immediate response.

source: We analyzed the short-term and long-term outcome of 42 patients with distal type aortic dissection.
target: This study evaluated the short- and long-term outcome of 42 patients with distal type aortic dissection. Distal type aortic dissection is when an injury occurs to the inner layer of the aorta (main artery of the body), and blood flows between the layers of the aortic wall.

source: Twenty-eight patients underwent intensive medical therapy within two weeks after the onset of pain (acute dissection).
target: Twenty-eight patients underwent intensive medical therapy within two weeks after the onset of pain (acute dissection).

source: The remaining 14 patients had chronic dissection.
target: The remaining 14 patients had chronic dissection, meaning they were treated after more than 2 weeks had passed since pain onset (started).

source: The goals of medical treatment were to control blood pressure and to attain a negative C-reactive protein test result.
target: The goals of treatment were to control blood pressure and to attain a negative (no detected) C-reactive protein test result. C-reactive protein is a type of protein found in the blood that increases during times of internal inflammation (redness and swelling from infection-fighting).

source: Hospital survival rate in the patients with acute dissection was 96% (27/28).
target: Hospital survival rate in the patients with acute dissection was 96% (27/28).

source: In-hospital complications included changes in mental status, renal dysfunction, bradycardia, orthostatic hypotension, and liver dysfunction, all of which were managed medically.
target: There were several complications that occurred in-hospital during treatment. These complications include changes in mental status, kidney dysfunction, bradycardia (low heart rate), low blood pressure, and liver dysfunction. All of these complications were managed medically.

source: Three of these patients underwent surgical therapy in the chronic phase and were discharged uneventfully.
target: Three of these patients underwent surgical therapy in the chronic phase and were discharged without issue.

source: Fifteen (62.5%) of the 24 medically treated patients were discharged with negative C-reactive protein tests.
target: Fifteen of the 24 medically treated patients were discharged with negative C-reactive protein tests.

source: Spontaneous resolution of a dissection was demonstrated by radiological examinations in 8 cases.
target: Spontaneous resolution of a dissection was demonstrated by x-rays in 8 cases.

source: Five-year survival rates in 24 medically treated patients was 93%.
target: Nearly all of the 24 medically treated patients had five-year survival rates.

source: Hospital survival rate in the patients with chronic dissection was 100% (14/14).
target: Hospital survival rate in the patients with chronic dissection was 100%.

source: The rigorous control of blood pressure in the acute phase, and subsequent meticulous evaluation of the dissection by radiological tests and C-reactive protein test provides acceptable short-term and long-term outcomes of patients with acute distal dissection without the need for emergency surgical intervention.
target: Strict control of blood pressure and evaluation of the dissection by x-rays and C-reactive protein tests provide acceptable outcomes of patients with acute distal dissection. These measures eliminate the need for emergency surgical intervention.

source: We analyzed the short-term and long-term outcome of 42 patients with distal type aortic dissection.
target: We analyzed the short- and long-term outcome of 42 patients with distal type aortic dissection (a tear in the major blood vessel near the heart).

source: Twenty-eight patients underwent intensive medical therapy within two weeks after the onset of pain (acute dissection).
target: Twenty-eight patients underwent intense treatment within two weeks after the start of pain (acute dissection or immediate rupture).

source: The remaining 14 patients had chronic dissection.
target: The remaining 14 patients had long-lasting dissection or gradual breakdown of the major blood vessel near the heart.
