target: The distribution of clinical physical effects in patients carrying SCN1A mutations was as follows: twelve patients had classical DS, three patients had GEFS + syndrome and two relatives of DS patients were suffering from fever-related seizures.

source: Conclusions: Our study highlights the phenotypic and genotypic heterogeneities of DS and GEFS + with the important aim of gaining a deeper understanding of SCN1A-related disorders.
target: In conclusion, our study highlights the physical effects and gene-related differences of DS and GEFS + with the important aim of gaining a deeper understanding of SCN1A-related disorders.

source: This study also represents the first genetic analysis of the SCN1A gene in a Hungarian cohort with the DS and GEFS + syndrome phenotype.
target: This study also represents the first genetic analysis of the SCN1A gene in a Hungarian group with the DS and GEFS + syndrome physical make-up.

source: Background: SCN1 A is one of the most important epilepsy-related genes, with pathogenic variants leading to a range of phenotypes with varying disease severity.
target: Background: SCN1 A is one of the most important epilepsy-related genes, with disease-causing variants leading to a range of physical effects with varying disease severity.

source: Different modifying factors have been hypothesized to influence SCN1A-related phenotypes.
target: Different attributes have been hypothesized to influence SCN1A-related physical effects.

source: We investigate the presence of rare and more common variants in epilepsy-related genes as potential modifiers of SCN1A-related disease severity.
target: We investigate the presence of rare and more common gene types in epilepsy-related genes as possible influencers of SCN1A-related disease severity.

source: Methods: 87 patients with SCN1A-related epilepsy were investigated.
target: In the study's methods, 87 patients with SCN1A-related epilepsy were investigated.

source: Whole-exome sequencing was performed by the Beijing Genomics Institute (BGI).
target: Whole-body gene labeling was performed by the Beijing Genomics Institute (BGI).

source: Functional variants in 422 genes associated with epilepsy and/or neuronal excitability were investigated.
target: Functional gene types in 422 genes linked with epilepsy and/or brain excitability were checked.

source: Differences in proportions of variants between the epilepsy genes and four control gene sets were calculated, and compared to the proportions of variants in the same genes in the ExAC database.
target: Differences in amounts of gene types between the epilepsy genes and four unrelated gene sets were calculated and compared to the amounts of gene types in the same genes in a specific gene database.

source: Results: Statistically significant excesses of variants in epilepsy genes were observed in the complete cohort and in the combined group of mildly and severely affected patients, particularly for variants with minor allele frequencies of <0.05.
target: The study results show that excesses of gene types in epilepsy genes were observed in the complete group and in the combined group of mildly and severely affected patients, espeically for gene types with minor gene frequencies of <0.05.

source: Patients with extreme phenotypes showed much greater excesses of epilepsy gene variants than patients with intermediate phenotypes.
target: Patients with severe physical effects showed much greater excesses of epilepsy gene types than patients with medium physical effects.

source: Conclusion: Our results indicate that relatively common variants in epilepsy genes, which would not necessarily be classified as pathogenic, may play a large role in modulating SCN1A phenotypes.
target: In conclusion, our results show that relatively common gene types in epilepsy genes, which would not necessarily be classified as disease-causing, may play a large role in influencing SCN1A physical effects.

source: They may modify the phenotypes of both severely and mildly affected patients.
target: They may influence the physical effects of both severely and mildly affected patients.

source: Our results may be a first step toward meaningful testing of modifier gene variants in regular diagnostics for individual patients, to provide a better estimation of disease severity for newly diagnosed patients.
target: Our results may be a first step toward meaningful testing of influencing gene types in regular diagnostics for individual patients, to provide a better estimation of disease severity for newly identified patients.

source: Objective: To describe the spectrum of being detected gene mutations in patients with epilepsy in clinical practice of neurologists specializing in epilepsy with an analysis of diagnosed epileptic syndromes, the characteristics of seizures, the timing of a genetic diagnosis, options and treatment effectiveness.
target: We aim to describe the spectrum of detected gene mutations in patients with epilepsy (brain disorder with seizures) in clinical practice of brain physicians specializing in epilepsy with an analysis of identified epileptic diseases, the characteristics of seizures, the timing of a genetic diagnosis, options and treatment effectiveness.

source: Patients and methods: The study included 100 patients (40 boys, 60 girls) with epilepsy and/or epileptic encephalopathy and a gene mutation identified.
target: The study included 100 patients (40 boys, 60 girls) with epilepsy and/or epileptic encephalopathy (epilepsy that leads to brain damage) and a gene mutation identified.

source: The average age was 6.9Â±5.1 years.
target: The average age was 6.9Â±5.1 years.

source: Through remote access, epilepsy specialists filled out a specially designed unified table containing information from outpatient case history.
target: Through remote access, epilepsy specialists filled out a specially designed data table containing information from patient history.

source: Results and discussion: There are patients with a wide range of gene mutations, the leading of which is a mutation in the SCN1A gene (15%).
target: The results show there are patients with a wide range of gene mutations, the leading of which is a mutation in the specific gene called SCN1A (15%).

source: The main method (85%) of detection remains the sequencing of the last generation in the Â«Hereditary EpilepsyÂ» panel.
target: The main method (85%) of detection remains the gene labeling of the last group in the Â«Hereditary EpilepsyÂ panel, groups of genes linked with epilepsy.

source: Years pass from the onset of the disease to the genetic diagnosis (Me - 3 years).
target: Years pass from the start of the disease to the genetic diagnosis (average number is 3 years).

source: In most cases, patients with severe (52% have epileptic encephalopathy, 88% have developmental disorders) and pharmacoresistant (mean amount of anti-epileptic drugs - 3.8Â±2.2, multitherapy - 70%) syndromes have undergone genetic testing.
target: In most cases, patients with severe (52% have epileptic encephalopathy, 88% have growth disorders) and drug-resistant (average amount of anti-epileptic drugs - 3.8Â±2.2, multi-treatment - 70%) diseases have undergone genetic testing.

source: In the treatment of these patients epileptologists are increasingly (52%) use alternative methods: steroids, ketogenic diet and others.
target: In the treatment of these patients, epilepsy specialists are increasingly (52%) using alternative methods: steroids, ketogenic (low-carb) diet and others.

source: The absence of seizures was observed only in 46% of patients.
target: The lack of seizures was observed only in 46% of patients.

source: Conclusion: Thus, in the outpatient practice of epileptologists, patients with a wide range of gene mutations are found.
target: In conclusion, in the practice of epilepsy specialists, patients with a wide range of gene mutations are found.

source: As a rule, these are patients with severe, therapy-resistant epileptic syndromes.
target: As a rule, these are patients with severe, treatment-resistant epileptic diseases.

source: Background: Arthrogryposis multiplex congenita (AMC) is characterised by congenital joint contractures in two or more body areas.
target: Background: Arthrogryposis multiplex congenita (AMC) is characterised by deforemed, rigid joints at birth in two or more body areas.

source: AMC exhibits wide phenotypic and genetic heterogeneity.
target: AMC exhibits wide physical attributes and genetic types.

source: Our goals were to improve the genetic diagnosis rates of AMC, to evaluate the added value of whole exome sequencing (WES) compared with targeted exome sequencing (TES) and to identify new genes in 315 unrelated undiagnosed AMC families.
target: Our goals were to improve the genetic identification rates of AMC, to test the added value of whole gene labeling compared with targeted gene labeling and to identify new genes in 315 unrelated undiagnosed AMC families.

source: Methods: Several genomic approaches were used including genetic mapping of disease loci in multiplex or consanguineous families, TES then WES.
target: Several gene-related tools were used including gene mapping of disease-related genes in various groups, first in targeted gene labeling and then whole gene labeling.

source: Sanger sequencing was performed to identify or validate variants.
target: Gene sequencing was performed to identify or verify gene variants.

source: Results: We achieved disease gene identification in 52.7% of AMC index patients including nine recently identified genes (CNTNAP1, MAGEL2, ADGRG6, ADCY6, GLDN, LGI4, LMOD3, UNC50 and SCN1A).
target: We achieved disease gene identification in 52.7% of AMC patients including nine recently identified genes (CNTNAP1, MAGEL2, ADGRG6, ADCY6, GLDN, LGI4, LMOD3, UNC50 and SCN1A).

source: Moreover, we identified pathogenic variants in ASXL3 and STAC3 expanding the phenotypes associated with these genes.
target: Moreover, we identified disease-causing variants in ASXL3 and STAC3, expanding the physical attributes linked with these genes.

source: The most frequent cause of AMC was a primary involvement of skeletal muscle (40%) followed by brain (22%).
target: The most frequent cause of AMC was linked to skeletal muscle (40%) followed by brain (22%).

source: The most frequent mode of inheritance is autosomal recessive (66.3% of patients).
target: The most frequent mode of inheritance is from both parents (66.3% of patients).

source: In sporadic patients born to non-consanguineous parents (n=60), de novo dominant autosomal or X linked variants were observed in 30 of them (50%).
target: In irregular patients born to non-related parents (n=60), new, inherited or sex chromosome linked types were observed in 30 of them (50%).

source: Conclusion: New genes recently identified in AMC represent 21% of causing genes in our cohort.
target: In conclusion, new genes recently identified in AMC represent 21% of causing genes in our group.

source: A high proportion of de novo variants were observed indicating that this mechanism plays a prominent part in this developmental disease.
target: A high amount of new gene types were observed, indicating that genes plays a prominent part in this developmental disease.

source: Our data showed the added value of WES when compared with TES due to the larger clinical spectrum of some disease genes than initially described and the identification of novel genes.
target: Our data showed the added value of whole gene labeling when compared with targeted gene labeling due to the larger medical variance of some disease genes than expected and the identification of new genes.

source: Neurodevelopmental diseases are increasingly recognized to be caused by "de novo" variants with the expanding use of next-generation sequencing.
target: Brain-related development (neurodevelopment) diseases are increasingly recognized to be caused by new gene mutations (de novo variants) with the use of state-of-the-art gene labeling.

source: The apparent de novo variants may actually be low-level hereditary parental mosaic variants, which could increase the recurrence risk of disease by >50% and is thought to be an underappreciated cause of neurodevelopmental diseases.
target: These de novo variants may actually be inherited, which could increase the reapparance risk of disease by >50% and is thought to be an underappreciated cause of neurodevelopment diseases.

source: Our study aimed to investigate the frequency of parental mosaicism in "de novo" neurodevelopmental diseases.
target: Our study aimed to find the amount of inheritance in "de novo" neurodevelopmental diseases.

source: A total of 237 patients (and parents) with neurodevelopmental diseases carrying apparent de novo pathogenic or likely pathogenic variants were recruited consecutively.
target: A total of 237 patients (and parents) with neurodevelopmental diseases carrying apparent de novo disease-causing (pathogenic) or likely pathogenic variants were included.

source: Deep next-generation sequencing was performed on parental samples to identify parental mosaicism.
target: Gene labeling was performed on parental samples to identify parental gene make-up.

source: Fourteen parental disease-causing mosaicism variants (3.0%) in 11 genes were detected with alternate allele frequency (AAF) 0.22%-34%.
target: Fourteen parental disease-causing gene types (3.0%) in 11 genes were detected with the amount of gene types ranging from 0.22%-34%.

source: Three parents showed milder clinical phenotypes than their offspring with relatively high AAF (23.33%, 25%, 34% separately).
target: Three parents showed milder disease-related physical attributes than their offspring with relatively high amounts of gene types (23.33%, 25%, 34% separately).

source: One recurrent variant was identified prenatally.
target: One reoccuring gene type was identified before birth.

source: A review of cohort study on parental mosaicism in neurodevelopmental diseases was performed.
target: A review of group study on gene inheritance in neurodevelopmental diseases was performed.

source: Our study highlights that identifying the parental mosaic disease-causing variants especially the low-level mosaicism will contribute to improving the accuracy of genetic counseling and prenatal diagnosis for reproductive risks.
target: Our study highlights that identifying the inherited disease-causing gene types will contribute to improving the accuracy of genetic treatment and before-birth diagnosis for reproductive risks.

source: Background: Glucose-transporter-1 deficiency syndrome (GLUT1-DS), due to SLC2A1 gene mutation, is characterized by early-onset seizures, which are often drug-resistant, developmental delay, and hypotonia.
target: Background: Glucose-transporter-1 deficiency syndrome (GLUT1-DS), an inability to transport sugar in the blood due to a change in the specific gene SLC2A1, is characterized by early-onset seizures, which are often drug-resistant, delay proper growth, and reduce muscle tone.

source: Hemiplegic migraine (HM) is a rare form of migraine, defined by headache associated with transient hemiplegia, and can be caused by mutations in either CACNA1A, ATP1A2, or SCN1A.
target: Hemiplegic migraine (HM) is a rare form of migraine, defined by headache linked with temporary paralysis on one side of the body (hemiplegia). It can be caused by mutations in either CACNA1A, ATP1A2, or SCN1A, specific genes.

source: Paroxysmal movements, other transient neurological disorders, or hemiplegic events can occur in GLUT1-DS patients with a mild phenotype.
target: Paroxysmal movements (or suddent fits), other temporary brain-related disorders, or hemiplegic events can occur in GLUT1-DS patients with mild effects.

source: Case: We report on a girl with GLUT1-DS, due to SLC2A1 mutation, with a mild phenotype.
target: We describe a girl with GLUT1-DS, due to SLC2A1 mutation, with mild effects.

source: In early childhood, she developed epilepsy and mild cognitive impairment, balance disorders, and clumsiness.
target: In early childhood, she developed epilepsy and mild intelligence impairment, balance disorders, and clumsiness.

source: At the age of 9, the patient reported a first hemiplegic episode, which regressed spontaneously.
target: At the age of 9, the patient reported a first hemiplegic episode, which healed randomly.

source: Over the next 3 years, two similar episodes occurred, accompanied by headache.
target: Over the next 3 years, two similar episodes occurred, followed by headache.

source: Therefore, in the hypothesis of HM, genetic testing was performed and CACNA1A mutation was identified.
target: Therefore, in the theory of HM, genetic testing was performed and a specific CACNA1A gene mutation was found.

source: The treatment with Lamotrigine avoided the recurrence of HM episodes.
target: The treatment with Lamotrigine (anti-seizure medication) avoided the reapparance of HM episodes.

source: Discussion: To our knowledge, among the several cases of GLUT1-DS with HM symptoms described in the literature, genetic testing was only performed in two of them, which eventually proved to be negative.
target: To our knowledge, among the several cases of GLUT1-DS with HM symptoms in the medical studies, genetic testing was only performed in two of them, which eventually found nothing.

source: In all other cases, no other genes except for SLC2A1 were examined.
target: In all other cases, no other genes except for SLC2A1 were examined.

source: Consequently, our patient would be the first description of GLUT1-DS with HM due to CACNA1A mutation.
target: Thus, our patient would be the first description of GLUT1-DS with HM due to the CACNA1A gene mutation.

source: We would emphasize the importance of performing specific genetic testing in patients with GLUT1-DS with symptoms evocative of HM, which may allow clinicians to use specific pharmacotherapy.
target: We would highlihgt the importance of performing specific genetic testing in patients with GLUT1-DS with symptoms similar to HM, which may allow clinicians to use specific drugs.

source: Background and purpose: In childhood epilepsy, genetic etiology is increasingly recognized in recent years with the advent of next generation sequencing.
target: For the study's background, in childhood epilepsy, genetic causes are increasingly recognized in recent years with the use of gene labeling or sequencing.

source: This has broadened the scope of precision medicine in intractable epilepsy, particularly epileptic encephalopathy (EE).
target: This has increased the scope of individualized medicine in unmanageable epileptic seizures or epilepsy, particularly epileptic encephalopathy (EE) (epilepsy that damages the brain).

source: Developmental disorder (DD) is an integral part of childhood uncontrolled epilepsy.
target: Developmental disorder (DD) (impairments in a child's growth) is an important part of childhood uncontrolled epilepsy.

source: This study was performed to investigate the genetic etiology of childhood epilepsy and DD.
target: This study was performed to measure the genetic causes of childhood epilepsy and DD.

source: Methods: In this study, 40 children with epilepsy and DD with positive genetic mutation were included retrospectively.
target: In this study, 40 children with epilepsy and DD with genetic mutations were included via prior records.

source: It was done in a tertiary care referral hospital of Bangladesh from January 2019 to December 2020.
target: It was done in a specialized care hospital of Bangladesh from January 2019 to December 2020.

source: Genetic study was done by next generation sequencing.
target: Genetic study was done by state-of-the-art gene labeling or sequencing.

source: In all cases electroencephalography, neuroimaging was done and reviewed.
target: In all cases electroencephalography (measuring electrical acitvity of the brain) and neuroimaging (imaging the brain) was done and reviewed.

source: Results: In total, 40 children were enrolled and the average age was 41.4Â±35.850 months with a male predominance (67.5%).
target: In total, 40 children were enrolled. The average age was 41.4Â±35.850 months with a male majority (67.5%).

source: Generalized seizure was the predominant type of seizure.
target: Generalized seizure was the main type of seizure.

source: Regarding the association, intellectual disability and attention deficit hyperactivity disorder was common.
target: Regarding the links, reduced intelligence and attention deficit hyperactivity disorder or ADHD was common.

source: Seventeen cases had genetically identified early infantile EE and common mutations observed were SCN1A (3), SCN8A (2), SLC1A2 (2), KCNT1 (2), and etc.
target: Seventeen cases had genetically identified EE developed during infancy. Common gene mutations observed were SCN1A (3), SCN8A (2), SLC1A2 (2), KCNT1 (2), and etc (genes linked with epilepsy).

source: Five patients of progressive myoclonic epilepsy were diagnosed and the mutations identified were in KCTD7, MFSD8, and CLN6 genes.
target: Five patients of worsening, muscle-jerk-related epilepsy were identified. The mutations identified were in KCTD7, MFSD8, and CLN6 genes.

source: Three cases had mitochondrial gene mutation (MT-ND5, MT-CYB).
target: Three cases had mitochondrial gene mutation (MT-ND5, MT-CYB) (genes that affect the mitochondria or the powerhouse of the cell).

source: Some rare syndromes like Gibbs syndrome, KohlschÃ¼tter-TÃ¶nz syndrome, Cockayne syndrome, Pitt-Hopkins syndrome and cerebral creatine deficiency were diagnosed.
target: Some rare diseases like Gibbs syndrome (brain disorder with weak muscle tone), KohlschÃ¼tter-TÃ¶nz syndrome (genetic disorder with seizures), Cockayne syndrome (delayed development disorder), Pitt-Hopkins syndrome (child development disorder) and cerebral creatine deficiency (creatine-metabolizing disorder) were identified.

source: Conclusions: This is the first study from Bangladesh on genetics of epilepsy and DD.
target: In conclusion, this is the first study from Bangladesh on genetics of epilepsy and DD.

source: This will help to improve the understanding of genetics epilepsy of this region as well as contribute in administering precision medicine in these patients.
target: This will help to improve the understanding of genetics epilepsy of this region and contribute in giving individualized medicine in these patients.

source: Pathogenic variants in the voltage-gated sodium channel gene (SCN1A) are amongst the most common genetic causes of childhood epilepsies.
target: Disease-causing variants in the sodium transporter gene (SCN1A) are amongst the most common genetic causes of childhood epilepsies (a brain disorder leading to seizures).

source: There is considerable heterogeneity in both the types of causative variants and associated phenotypes; a recent expansion of the phenotypic spectrum of SCN1A associated epilepsies now includes an early onset severe developmental and epileptic encephalopathy with regression and a hyperkinetic movement disorder.
target: There is considerable variation in both the types of causative gene types and associated physical effects. A recent increase of the physical effects of SCN1A-related epilepsies now includes an early severe growth and epileptic encephalopathy (epilepsy leading to brain damage) with reduction in growth and a abnormal movement disorder.

source: Herein, we report a female with a developmental and degenerative epileptic-dyskinetic encephalopathy, distinct and more severe than classic Dravet syndrome.
target: We report a female with a growth-related and worsening epileptic-movement-related encephalopathy, distinct and more severe than classic Dravet syndrome (lifelong epilepsy).

source: Clinical diagnostics indicated a paternally inherited c.5053G>T; p. A1685S variant of uncertain significance in SCN1A.
target: Medical diagnostics indicated a inherited gene type of SCN1A.

source: Whole-exome sequencing detected a second de novo mosaic (18%) c.2345G>A; p. T782 I likely pathogenic variant in SCN1A (maternal allele).
target: Whole-body gene labeling found a second inherited, likely disease-causing gene type of SCN1A.

source: Biophysical characterization of both mutant channels in a heterologous expression system identified gain-of-function effects in both, with a milder shift in fast inactivation of the p. A1685S channels; and a more severe persistent sodium current in the p. T782I.
target: Biophysical measurements of both mutant gene types found gain-of-function effects in both SCN1A products; and a more severe persistent physical effect from the second gene type.

source: Using computational models, we show that large persistent sodium currents induce hyper-excitability in individual cortical neurons, thus relating the severe phenotype to the empirically quantified sodium channel dysfunction.
target: Using mathematical analysis, we show that large persistent electrical activity from sodium in the body causes hyper-excitability in brain neurons, thus linking the severe physical effects to the sodium current dysfunction.

source: These findings further broaden the phenotypic spectrum of SCN1A associated epilepsies and highlight the importance of testing for mosaicism in epileptic encephalopathies.
target: These findings further broaden the spectrum of physical effects of SCN1A linked epilepsies and highlight the importance of testing for inheritance in epileptic encephalopathies.

source: Detailed biophysical evaluation and computational modelling further highlight the role of gain-of-function variants in the pathophysiology of the most severe phenotypes associated with SCN1A.
target: Detailed biophysical tests and mathematical analysis further highlight the role of gain-of-function gene types in the disease-causing attributes of the most severe physical effects linked with SCN1A.

source: Background and objectives: Pathogenic variants in the neuronal sodium-channel Î±1-subunit gene (SCN1A) are the most frequent monogenic cause of epilepsy.
target: For the study's background, we studied disease-causing types of the brain-located gene called SCN1A, which is the most frequent cause of epileptic seizures or epilepsy.

source: Phenotypes comprise a wide clinical spectrum including the severe childhood epilepsy, Dravet syndrome, characterized by drug-resistant seizures, intellectual disability and high mortality, and the milder genetic epilepsy with febrile seizures plus (GEFS+), characterized by normal cognition.
target: Physical attributes make up a wide spectrum including the severe childhood epilepsy, Dravet syndrome, epilepsy characterized by drug-resistant seizures, lower intelligence, and high death risk, and the milder genetic epilepsy with fever-like seizures plus (GEFS+), characterized by normal intelligence.

source: Early recognition of a child's risk for developing Dravet syndrome versus GEFS+ is key for implementing disease-modifying therapies when available before cognitive impairment emerges.
target: Early identification of a child's risk for developing Dravet syndrome versus GEFS+ is key for giving disease-modifying treatments when available before intelligence impairment come up.

source: Our objective was to develop and validate a prediction model using clinical and genetic biomarkers for early diagnosis of SCN1A-related epilepsies.
target: Our objective was to develop and verify a disease prediction model using medical and genetic biomarkers for early identification of SCN1A-related epilepsies.

source: Methods: Retrospective multicenter cohort study comprising data from SCN1A-positive Dravet syndrome and GEFS+ patients consecutively referred for genetic testing (March 2001-June 2020) including age of seizure onset and a newly-developed SCN1A genetic score.
target: For the study's methods, it was a multicenter group study comprising data from previous SCN1A-positive Dravet syndrome and GEFS+ patients consistently treated with genetic testing (March 2001-June 2020). Data includes age of seizure start and a newly-developed SCN1A genetic score.

source: A training cohort was used to develop multiple prediction models that were validated using two independent blinded cohorts.
target: A training group was used to develop multiple disease prediction models that were verified using two independent groups.

source: Primary outcome was the discriminative accuracy of the model predicting Dravet syndrome versus other GEFS+ phenotypes.
target: The main measure was the accuracy of the model predicting Dravet syndrome versus other GEFS+ phenotypes.

source: Results: 1018 participants were included.
target: 1018 participants were included.

source: The frequency of Dravet syndrome was 616/743 (83%) in the training cohort, 147/203 (72%) in validation cohort 1 and 60/72 (83%) in validation cohort 2.
target: The frequency of Dravet syndrome was 616/743 (83%) in the training group, 147/203 (72%) in test group 1 and 60/72 (83%) in test group 2.

source: A high SCN1A genetic score 133.4 (SD, 78.5) versus 52.0 (SD, 57.5; p < 0.001) and young age of onset 6.0 (SD, 3.0) months versus 14.8 (SD, 11.8; p < 0.001) months, were each associated with Dravet syndrome versus GEFS+.
target: A high SCN1A genetic score - 133.4 versus 52.0 - and young age of start - 6.0 months versus 14.8 months -  were each linked with Dravet syndrome versus GEFS+.

source: A combined 'SCN1A genetic score and seizure onset' model separated Dravet syndrome from GEFS+ more effectively (area under the curve [AUC], 0.89 [95% CI, 0.86-0.92]) and outperformed all other models (AUC, 0.79-0.85; p < 0.001).
target: A combined 'SCN1A genetic score and seizure onset' model separated Dravet syndrome from GEFS+ more effectively and outperformed all other models.

source: Model performance was replicated in both validation cohorts 1 (AUC, 0.94 [95% CI, 0.91-0.97]) and 2 (AUC, 0.92 [95% CI, 0.82-1.00]).
target: Model performance was replicated in both test group 1 and 2.

source: Discussion: The prediction model allows objective estimation at disease onset whether a child will develop Dravet syndrome versus GEFS+, assisting clinicians with prognostic counseling and decisions on early institution of precision therapies (http://scn1a-prediction-model.broadinstitute.org/).
target: In short, the prediction model allows measurable estimation at disease start whether a child will develop Dravet syndrome versus GEFS+, helping clinicians with treatment counseling and decisions on early use of specific treatments.

source: Classification of evidence: This study provides Class II evidence that a combined 'SCN1A genetic score and seizure onset' model distinguishes Dravet syndrome from other GEFS+ phenotypes.
target: This study provides strong evidence that a combined 'SCN1A genetic score and seizure onset' model identifies Dravet syndrome from other GEFS+ phenotypes.

source: Background: The EPIGENE network was created in 2014 by four multidisciplinary teams composed of geneticists, pediatric neurologists and neurologists specialized in epileptology and neurophysiology.
target: As the article's background, the EPIGENE network was created in 2014 by four multi-specialty teams made up of geneticists, neurologists treating young children and neurologists specialized in epileptic seizures (or epilepsy) and brain make-up.

source: The ambition of the network was to harmonize and improve the diagnostic strategy of Mendelian epileptic disorders using next-generation sequencing, in France.
target: The goal of the network was to group together and improve the identification strategy of genetic epileptic disorders using state-of-the-art gene tagging, in France.

source: Over the years, five additional centers have joined EPIGENE and the network has been working in close collaboration, since 2018, with the French reference center for rare epilepsies (CRÃ©ER).
target: Over the years, five additional centers have joined EPIGENE. The network has been working closely, since 2018, with the French reference center for rare epilepsies (CRÃ©ER).

source: Results: Since 2014, biannual meetings have led to the design of four successive versions of a monogenic epilepsy gene panel (PAGEM), increasing from 68 to 144 genes.
target: Since 2014, biannual meetings have created four successive versions of a single-gene epilepsy gene panel (PAGEM), increasing from 68 to 144 genes.

source: A total of 4035 index cases with epileptic disorders have been analyzed with a diagnostic yield of 31% (n = 1265/4035).
target: A total of 4035 patient cases with epileptic disorders have been analyzed with an identification accuracy of 31% (n = 1265/4035).

source: The top 10 genes, SCN1A, KCNQ2, STXBP1, SCN2A, SCN8A, PRRT2, PCDH19, KCNT1, SYNGAP1, and GRIN2A, account for one-sixth of patients and half of the diagnoses provided by the PAGEM.
target: The top 10 epileptic-related genes, SCN1A, KCNQ2, STXBP1, SCN2A, SCN8A, PRRT2, PCDH19, KCNT1, SYNGAP1, and GRIN2A, account for one-sixth of patients and half of the identifications provided by the PAGEM.

source: Conclusion: These results suggest that a gene-panel approach is an efficient first-tier test for the genetic diagnosis of Mendelian epileptic disorders.
target: In conclusion, these results suggest that a gene-panel approach is an efficient, gold-standard test for the genetic identification of genetic epileptic disorders.

source: In a near future, French patients with "drug-resistant epilepsies with seizure-onset in the first two-years of life" can benefit from whole-genome sequencing (WGS), as a second line genetic screening with the implementation of the 2025 French Genomic Medicine Plan.
target: In a near future, French patients with "drug-resistant epilepsies with seizure-onset in the first two-years of life" can benefit from tagging their entire genome, as a secondary genetic screening with the use of the 2025 French Genomic Medicine Plan.

source: The EPIGENE network has also promoted scientific collaborations on genetic epilepsies within CRÃ©ER.
target: The EPIGENE network has also promoted scientific partnerships on genetic epilepsies within CRÃ©ER.

source: There is evidence that alteration in plasma fatty acid composition may play a role in certain neurological disorders.
target: There is evidence that changes in blood fatty acid make-up may play a role in certain brain disorders.

source: This case control study was conducted to evaluate the association between plasma fatty acid levels and mental retardation in Korean children.
target: This patient study was conducted to test the link between blood fatty acid levels and mental retardation in Korean children.

source: Plasma phospholipid fatty acids, plasma lipids, dietary fatty acids and selected nutrients were measured in 31 mentally retarded boys (mean age 9.93 +/-1.5 yrs) and matched controls.
target: Blood fatty acids, blood fat levels, dietary fatty acids and selected nutrients were measured in 31 mentally retarded boys (average age 9.93 +/-1.5 yrs) and normal boys.

source: Total plasma omega-3 fatty acids (Sigmaw3), docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) and high density lipoprotein (HDL) concentrations were significantly lower and the Sigmaomega-6/Sigmaomega-3 ratio was significantly higher in cases than in controls.
target: Total blood omega-3 fatty acids (Sigmaw3), docosahexaenoic acid (DHA; specific omega-3 fatty acids) and high density lipoprotein (HDL - good cholesterol) concentrations were much lower. The Sigmaomega-6/Sigmaomega-3 ratio was much higher in affectedpatients than in normal patients.

source: The odds in favor of mental retardation increased by 69 % for each unit increase in the Sigmaomega-6/ Sigmaomega-3 ratio (adjusted odds ratio = 1.69, 95% CI = 1.25-2.29).
target: The odds in favor of mental retardation increased by 69 % for each unit increase in the Sigmaomega-6/ Sigmaomega-3 ratio.

source: Significant variation in plasma Sigmaomega-3 and the Sigmaomega-6/ Sigmaomega-3 ratio was explained by mental retardation and plasma HDL concentrations (45% and 37 % respectively).
target: Significant variation in blood Sigmaomega-3 and the Sigmaomega-6/ Sigmaomega-3 ratio was explained by mental retardation and blood HDL concentrations (45% and 37 % respectively).

source: There was a significant inverse association between plasma DHA and mental retardation.
target: There was a significant opposite link between blood DHA and mental retardation. As one goes up, the other goes down.

source: For each unit increase in plasma DHA, odds of mental retardation decreased by 74 %.
target: For each unit increase in blood DHA, odds of mental retardation decreased by 74 %.

source: There was no significant difference in either total dietary fat or fatty acids intakes between cases and controls.
target: There was no significant difference in either total dietary fat or fatty acids intakes between affected patients and normal patients.

source: The energy intake of cases was significantly higher than the controls.
target: The energy intake of affected patients was significantly higher than the healthy patients.

source: These results suggest that proportion of plasma Sigmaomega-3 fatty acids, particularly, DHA, and the Sigmaomega-6/ Sigmaomega-3 ratio are associated with mental retardation in children in this study.
target: These results suggest that amount of plasma Sigmaomega-3 fatty acids, particularly, DHA, and the Sigmaomega-6/ Sigmaomega-3 ratio are linked with mental retardation in children in this study.

source: Background: The ω-6 (n-6) to ω-3 (n-3) fatty acid (FA) ratio (n-6:n-3 ratio) was previously shown to be a predictor of executive function performance in children aged 7-9 y.
target: As the study's background, the ω-6 (n-6) to ω-3 (n-3) fatty acid (FA) ratio (n-6:n-3 ratio) was previously shown to be a predictor of brain function performance in children aged 7-9 y.

source: Objective: We aimed to replicate and extend previous findings by exploring the role of the n-6:n-3 ratio in executive function performance.
target: For the objective, we aimed to replicate and extend previous findings by exploring the role of the n-6:n-3 ratio in brain function performance.

source: We hypothesized that there would be an interaction between n-3 and the n-6:n-3 ratio, with children with low n-3 performing best with a low ratio, and those with high n-3 performing best with a high ratio.
target: We hypothesized that there would be an link between n-3 and the n-6:n-3 ratio, with children with low n-3 performing best with a low ratio, and those with high n-3 performing best with a high ratio.

source: Design: Children were recruited on the basis of their consumption of n-6 and n-3 FAs.
target: For the study design, children were recruited on the basis of their consumption of n-6 and n-3 FAs.

source: The executive function performance of 78 children aged 7-12 y was tested with the use of the Cambridge Neuropsychological Test Automated Battery and a planning task.
target: The brain function performance of 78 children aged 7-12 y was tested with the use of specific brain health tests and a planning task.

source: Participants provided blood for plasma FA quantification, and the caregiver completed demographic and activity questionnaires.
target: Participants provided blood for FA quantification, and the caregiver completed basic background and activity questionnaires.

source: We investigated the role of the n-6:n-3 ratio in the entire sample and separately in children aged 7-9 y (n = 41) and 10-12 y (n = 37).
target: We investigated the role of the n-6:n-3 ratio in the entire sample and separately in children aged 7-9 y and 10-12 y.

source: Results: Dietary and plasma n-6:n-3 ratio and n-3 predicted performance on working memory and planning tasks in children 7-12 y old.
target: For the study results, diet and blood n-6:n-3 ratio and n-3 predicted performance on short-term memory and planning tasks in children 7-12 y old.

source: The interaction between dietary n-6:n-3 ratio and n-3 predicted the number of moves required to solve the most difficult planning problems in children aged 7-9 y and those aged 10-12 y, similar to results from the previous study.
target: The link between dietary n-6:n-3 ratio and n-3 predicted the number of moves required to solve the most difficult planning problems in children aged 7-9 y and those aged 10-12 y, similar to results from the previous study.

source: There was also an interaction between the plasma n-6:n-3 ratio and n-3 predicting time spent thinking through the difficult 5-move planning problems.
target: There was also a link between the blood n-6:n-3 ratio and n-3 predicting time spent thinking through the difficult 5-move planning problems.

source: The n-6:n-3 ratio and n-3 predicted executive function performance differently in children aged 7-9 y and in those aged 10-12 y, indicating different optimal FA balances across development.
target: The n-6:n-3 ratio and n-3 predicted brain function performance differently in children aged 7-9 y and in those aged 10-12 y, indicating different optimal FA balances across age.

source: Conclusions: The n-6:n-3 ratio is an important consideration in the role of FAs in cognitive function, and the optimal balance of n-6 and n-3 FAs depends on the cognitive function and developmental period studied.
target: In conclusion, the n-6:n-3 ratio is an important factor in the role of FAs in brain function. The optimal balance of n-6 and n-3 FAs depends on the brain function and age period studied.

source: Background: Attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) is a debilitating behavioural disorder affecting daily ability to function, learn, and interact with peers.
target: As the study's background, attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) is a harmful behavioural disorder affecting daily ability to function, learn, and interact with peers.

source: This publication assesses the role of omega-3/6 fatty acids in the treatment and management of ADHD.
target: This publication measures the role of omega-3/6 fatty acids in the treatment and management of ADHD.

source: Methods: A systematic review of 16 randomised controlled trials was undertaken.
target: A review of 16 randomised controlled trials was done.

source: Trials included a total of 1,514 children and young people with ADHD who were allocated to take an omega-3/6 intervention, or a placebo.
target: Trials included a total of 1,514 children and young people with ADHD who were given either an omega-3/6 treatment or a dummy treatment.

source: Results: Of the studies identified, 13 reported favourable benefits on ADHD symptoms including improvements in hyperactivity, impulsivity, attention, visual learning, word reading, and working/short-term memory.
target: Of the studies identified, 13 reported helpful benefits on ADHD symptoms including improvements in hyperactivity, impulsivity, attention, visual learning, word reading, and working/short-term memory.

source: Four studies used supplements containing a 9 : 3 : 1 ratio of eicosapentaenoic acid : docosahexaenoic acid : gamma linolenic acid which appeared effective at improving erythrocyte levels.
target: Four studies used supplements containing a 9 : 3 : 1 ratio of eicosapentaenoic acid (omega-3) : docosahexaenoic acid (omega-3) : gamma linolenic acid (omega-6) which appeared effective at improving red blood cell levels.

source: Supplementation with this ratio of fatty acids also showed promise as an adjunctive therapy to traditional medications, lowering the dose and improving the compliance with medications such as methylphenidate.
target: Supplementation with this ratio of fatty acids also showed promise as a secondary therapy to traditional medications, lowering the dose and improving the compliance with medications such as methylphenidate (stimulant to treat ADHD).

source: Conclusion: ADHD is a frequent and debilitating childhood condition.
target: In conclusion, ADHD is a frequent and harmful childhood condition.

source: Given disparaging feelings towards psychostimulant medications, omega-3/6 fatty acids offer great promise as a suitable adjunctive therapy for ADHD.
target: Given negative feelings towards psychostimulant medications, omega-3/6 fatty acids offer great promise as a suitable secondary therapy for ADHD.

source: Maternal obesity is associated with adverse offspring outcomes.
target: Maternal obesity is linked with harmful offspring outcomes.

source: Inflammation and deficiency of anti-inflammatory nutrients like omega(n)-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids (PUFA) may contribute to these associations.
target: Inflammation (infection-fighting processes) and lack of anti-inflammatory nutrients like omega(n)-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids (PUFA) may contribute to these links.

source: Fetal supply of n-3 PUFA is dependent on maternal levels and studies have suggested that improved offspring outcomes are associated with higher maternal intake.
target: Fetal supply of n-3 PUFA is dependent on maternal levels. Studies have suggested that improved offspring results are linked with higher maternal intake.

source: However, little is known about how maternal obesity affects the response to n-3 supplementation during pregnancy.
target: However, little is known about how maternal obesity affects the response to n-3 supplementation during pregnancy.

source: We sought to determine (1) the associations of obesity with PUFA concentrations and (2) if the systemic response to n-3 supplementation differs by body mass index (BMI).
target: We sought to determine (1) the links of obesity with PUFA concentrations and (2) if the full-body response to n-3 supplementation differs by body mass index (BMI).

source: This was a secondary analysis of 556 participants (46% lean, 28% obese) in the Maternal-Fetal Medicine Units Network trial of n-3 (Docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) + Eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA)) supplementation, in which participants had 2g/day of n-3 (n = 278) or placebo (n = 278) from 19 to 22 weeks until delivery.
target: This was a secondary analysis of 556 participants (46% lean, 28% obese) in the Maternal-Fetal Medicine Units Network trial of n-3 (Docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) + Eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA) - specific omega-3 fatty acids) supplementation, in which participants had 2g/day of n-3 or sham treatment from 19 to 22 weeks until delivery.

source: At baseline, obese women had higher plasma n-6 arachidonic acid concentrations (β: 0.96% total fatty acids; 95% Confidence Interval (CI): 0.13, 1.79) and n-6/n-3 ratio (β: 0.26 unit; 95% CI: 0.05, 0.48) compared to lean women.
target: At start, obese women had higher blood n-6 arachidonic acid (specific omega-6 fatty acid) concentrations and n-6/n-3 ratio compared to lean women.

source: In the adjusted analysis, women in all BMI groups had higher n-3 concentrations following supplementation, although obese women had attenuated changes (β = -2.04%, CI: -3.19, -0.90, interaction p = 0.000) compared to lean women, resulting in a 50% difference in the effect size.
target: In the adjusted analysis, women in all BMI groups had higher n-3 concentrations following supplementation, although obese women had reduced changes compared to lean women, resulting in a 50% difference.

source: Similarly, obese women also had an attenuated reduction (β = 0.94 units, CI: 0.40, 1.47, interaction p = 0.046) in the n-6/n-3 ratio (marker of inflammatory status), which was 65% lower compared to lean women.
target: Similarly, obese women also had a smaller reduction in the n-6/n-3 ratio (marker of inflammatory status), which was 65% lower compared to lean women.

source: Obesity is associated with higher inflammation and with an attenuated response to n-3 supplementation in pregnancy.
target: Obesity is linked with higher inflammation and with a weakened response to n-3 supplementation in pregnancy.

source: Evidence for a relationship between omega-6/omega-3 (n-6/n-3) polyunsaturated fatty acid (PUFA) ratio and obesity in humans is inconsistent, perhaps due to differences in dietary intake or metabolism of PUFAs between different subsets of the population.
target: Evidence for a link between omega-6/omega-3 (n-6/n-3) polyunsaturated fatty acid (PUFA) ratio and obesity in humans is inconsistent, likely due to differences in diet or metabolism of PUFAs between different groups.

source: Since chronic inflammation is central to obesity and inflammatory pathways are regulated by PUFAs, the objective of this study was to examine whether variants in the NFKB1 gene, an upstream regulator of the inflammatory response, modify the association between the n-6/n-3 ratio (from diet and plasma) and anthropometric traits in a multiethnic/multiracial population of young adults.
target: Since chronic (long-lasting) inflammation (infection-fighting processes) is central to obesity and inflammatory pathways are regulated by PUFAs, the objective of this study was to examine whether gene types in the NFKB1 gene, a regulator of the inflammatory response, modify the link between the n-6/n-3 ratio (from diet and blood) and anthropometric traits (human body proportions) in a multiethnic/multiracial group of young adults.

source: Participants' (n = 898) dietary PUFA intake was assessed using a food frequency questionnaire and plasma PUFA concentrations by gas chromatography.
target: Participants' (n = 898) diet PUFA intake was measured using a food frequency questionnaire and blood PUFA levels.

source: Nine tag single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNP) in NFKB1 were genotyped.
target: Nine gene mutations in NFKB1 were found.

source: Significant interactions were found between racial/ethnic groups and plasma n-6/n-3 ratio for body mass index (BMI) (p = 0.02) and waist circumference (WC) (p = 0.007).
target: Significant links were found between racial/ethnic groups and plasma n-6/n-3 ratio for body mass index (BMI) and waist circumference (WC).

source: Significant interactions were also observed between racial/ethnic groups and three NFKB1 genotypes (rs11722146, rs1609798, and rs230511) for BMI and WC (all p ≤ 0.04).
target: Significant links were also found between racial/ethnic groups and three NFKB1 gene types for BMI and WC.

source: Significant interactions were found between two NFKB1 genotypes and plasma n-6/n-3 ratio for BMI and WC (rs4648090 p = 0.02 and 0.03; rs4648022 p = 0.06 and 0.04, respectively).
target: Significant link were found between two NFKB1 genotypes and blood n-6/n-3 ratio for BMI and WC.

source: Our findings suggest that anthropometric traits may be influenced by a unique combination of n-6/n-3 ratio, racial/ethnic background, and NFKB1 genotypes.
target: Our findings suggest that anthropometric traits may be influenced by a special combination of n-6/n-3 ratio, racial/ethnic background, and NFKB1 gene ty[es.

source: Background: Atopic dermatitis is a common childhood disease, potentially influenced by prenatal nutritional exposures such as polyunsaturated fatty acids (PUFAs).
target: As background for this study, atopic dermatitis (eczema) is a common childhood disease, potentially influenced by pre-birth nutritional exposures such as polyunsaturated fatty acids (PUFAs).

source: Objective: In a racially diverse cohort, we hypothesized that childhood atopic dermatitis would be associated with higher prenatal omega-6 (n-6) and lower omega-3 (n-3) PUFAs.
target: As the objective for this study, in a racially diverse group, we hypothesized that childhood atopic dermatitis would be linked with higher pre-birth omega-6 (n-6) and lower omega-3 (n-3) PUFAs.

source: Methods: We included mother-child dyads, births 2006 to 2011, enrolled in the University of Tennessee Health Sciences Center Conditions Affecting Neurocognitive Development in Early Childhood cohort.
target: We included mother-child groups, births 2006 to 2011, enrolled in the University of Tennessee Health Sciences Center Conditions Affecting Neurocognitive (brain-related intelligence) Development in Early Childhood group.

source: Primary exposures included second trimester plasma n-3 and n-6 PUFA status and the ratio of the two (n-6:n-3).
target: Main events included second trimester blood n-3 and n-6 PUFA status and the ratio of the two (n-6:n-3).

source: We assessed child current atopic dermatitis symptoms in the previous 12 months at age approximately 4 to 6 years.
target: We measured child current atopic dermatitis symptoms in the previous 12 months of age approximately 4 to 6 years.

source: We investigated the association between PUFA exposures and atopic dermatitis using multivariable logistic regression, adjusting for potential confounders.
target: We investigated the link between PUFA exposures and atopic dermatitis using mathematical analysis.

source: We assessed for effect modification by maternal prenatal smoking, atopic disease history, and child sex.
target: We measured for effect modification by maternal pre-birth smoking, atopic (allergy-related) disease history, and child sex.

source: Results: Among 1131 women, 67% were African American and 42% had an atopic disease history; 17% of children had atopic dermatitis.
target: The results include among 1131 women, 67% were African American and 42% had an atopic disease history; 17% of children had atopic dermatitis.

source: Higher prenatal n-6 PUFAs were associated with increased relative odds of child atopic dermatitis (adjusted odds ratio: 1.25; confidence interval: 1.01-1.54 per interquartile range difference), and interaction models demonstrated that this association was seen in dyads in which the women had a history of atopic disease.
target: Higher pre-birth n-6 PUFAs were linked with increased relative odds of child atopic dermatitis, and statistical models show that this link was seen in two-member groups in which the women had a history of atopic disease.

source: Neither prenatal n-3 PUFAs nor n-6:n-3 were associated with child atopic dermatitis.
target: Neither pre-birth n-3 PUFAs nor n-6:n-3 were linked with child atopic dermatitis.

source: Conclusion: In this racially diverse cohort, higher second trimester n-6 PUFAs were associated with atopic dermatitis in children of women with atopy.
target: In conclusion, in this racially diverse cohort, higher second trimester n-6 PUFAs were linked with atopic dermatitis in children of women with atopy.

source: PUFAs may represent a modifiable risk factor for atopic dermatitis, particularly in individuals with a familial predisposition.
target: PUFAs may represent a modifiable risk factor for atopic dermatitis, especially in individuals with an inherited risk factor.

source: Background/aim: Breast cancer is the most common type of cancer among women around the world and the leading cause of cancer-related death among women.
target: For this study's background, breast cancer is the most common type of cancer among women around the world and the leading cause of cancer-related death among women.

source: The knowledge about modifiable risk factors, such as diet, can be an acceptable, cheap and non-pharmacological prevention tool.
target: The knowledge about changeable risk factors, such as diet, can be an acceptable, cheap and non-drug prevention tool.

source: The aim of this study was to investigate the association between dietary fat, dietary fatty acids, fish intake, and breast cancer in women.
target: The aim of this study was to check the link between dietary fat, dietary fatty acids, fish intake, and breast cancer in women.

source: Patients and methods: A case-control study was designed.
target: A patient study was designed.

source: A total of 201 consecutive, newly diagnosed, polish female cancer patients (mean age: 58 years) and 201 one-to-one age-matched controls were enrolled.
target: A total of 201 newly diagnosed, polish female cancer patients (average age: 58 years) and 201 age-matched patients without cancer were included.

source: A standardized questionnaire assessing various socio-demographic, clinical, lifestyle, and dietary characteristics was applied via face-to-face interviews.
target: A standard questionnaire measuring various socio-demographic, clinical, lifestyle, and dietary characteristics was applied via face-to-face interviews.

source: Detailed dietary intake information was assessed using a validated Food Frequency Questionnaire.
target: Detailed dietary intake information was measured using a verified diet questionnaire.

source: Odds ratios (OR) and 95% confidence intervals (95%CI) were obtained using multiple unconditional logistic regression models controlling for non-dietary and dietary potential confounders.
target: Results were obtained using statistical models controlling for non-dietary and dietary potential confounders.

source: Results: Consumption of polyunsaturated fats (PUFA) over 10% of total energy intake was associated with a significantly lower risk of breast cancer compared to low intake of PUFA (OR=0.4, 95%CI=0.19-0.85).
target: Consumption of polyunsaturated fats (PUFA) over 10% of total energy intake was linked with a significantly lower risk of breast cancer compared to low intake of PUFA.

source: Low (<0.2) omega-3/ omega-6 ratio (OR=2.04, 95%CI=0.996-4.17), fish consumption less than once every six months (OR=3.37, 95%CI=1.57-7.23) and being overweight (OR=2.07, 95%CI=1.3-3.3) were associated with increased risk of breast cancer.
target: Low (<0.2) omega-3/ omega-6 ratio, fish consumption less than once every six months and being overweight were linked with increased risk of breast cancer.

source: Residents of rural areas had a significantly higher risk compared to women from urban areas (OR=1.8, 95%CI=1.06-3.03).
target: Residents of rural areas had a much higher risk compared to women from urban areas.

source: Conclusion: High intake of PUFA can decrease the risk of breast cancer, while the low omega-3/omega-6 ratio increases the risk.
target: In conclusion, high intake of PUFA can decrease the risk of breast cancer, while the low omega-3/omega-6 ratio increases the risk.

source: In addition, overweight state, eliminating fish from the diet and living in rural areas can also increase the risk of breast cancer.
target: In addition, being overweight, eliminating fish from the diet and living in rural areas can also increase the risk of breast cancer.

source: Sickle cell disease (SCD) is a hematologic disorder with complex pathophysiology that includes chronic hemolysis, vaso-occlusion and inflammation.
target: Sickle cell disease (SCD) is a blood disorder with complex disease effects that includes lasting (chronic) hemolysis (red blood cell destruction), vaso-occlusion (blood flow blockage) and inflammation (infection-fighting processes).

source: Increased leukocyte-erythrocyte-endothelial interactions, due to upregulated expression of adhesion molecules and activated endothelium, are thought to play a primary role in initiation and progression of SCD vaso-occlusive crisis and end-organ damage.
target: Increased white blood cell-red blood cell-boundary cell interactions, due to increases expression of sticking molecules and activated boundary cells, are thought to play a main role in starting and developing of SCD vaso-occlusive events and organ damage.

source: Several new pathophysiology-based therapeutic options for SCD are being developed, chiefly targeting the inflammatory pathways.
target: Several new disease effect-based treatments for SCD are being developed, chiefly targeting the inflammatory pathways.

source: Omega-3 fatty acids are polyunsaturated fatty acids that are known to have effects on diverse physiological processes.
target: Omega-3 fatty acids are polyunsaturated fatty acids that are known to have effects on many biological processes.

source: Eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA) and docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) are the principal biologically active omega-3 fatty acids.
target: Eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA) and docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) are the main biologically active omega-3 fatty acids.

source: The therapeutic effects of DHA and EPA on chronic inflammatory disorders and cardiovascular diseases are well recognized.
target: The helpful effects of DHA and EPA on chronic inflammatory disorders and heart-related or cardiovascular diseases are well recognized.

source: The therapeutic effects of omega-3 fatty acids are attributed to their anti-inflammatory and anti-thrombotic eicosanoids, and the novel class of EPA and DHA derived lipid mediators: resolvins, protectins and maresins.
target: The helpful effects of omega-3 fatty acids are linked to their anti-inflammatory and anti-thrombotic (blood clotting) eicosanoids (signaling molecules), and the new class of EPA and DHA derived fat level influencers: resolvins, protectins and maresins (other fatty-acid-related molecules).

source: Blood cell membranes of patients with SCD have abnormal fatty acids composition characterized by high ratio of pro-inflammatory arachidonic acid (AA) to anti-inflammatory DHA and EPA (high omega-6/omega-3 ratio).
target: Blood cell membranes or boundaries of patients with SCD have abnormal fatty acids composition characterized by high ratio of pro-inflammatory arachidonic acid (AA) to anti-inflammatory DHA and EPA (high omega-6/omega-3 ratio).

source: In addition, experimental and clinical studies provide evidence that treatment with DHA does confer improvement in rheological properties of sickle RBC, inflammation and hemolysis.
target: Also, experimental and clinical studies provide evidence that treatment with DHA does lead to improvement in blood cell deforming properties of sickle RBC, inflammation and hemolysis.

source: The clinical studies have shown improvements in VOC rate, markers of inflammation, adhesion, and hemolysis.
target: The clinical studies have shown improvements in vaso-occlusion crisis (VOC) rate, markers of inflammation, sticking molecules, and hemolysis.

source: In toto, the results of studies on the therapeutic effects of omega-3 fatty acids in SCD provide good body of evidence that omega-3 fatty acids could be a safe and effective treatment for SCD.
target: In total, the results of studies on the helpful effects of omega-3 fatty acids in SCD provide a good body of evidence that omega-3 fatty acids could be a safe and effective treatment for SCD.

source: Polyunsaturated fatty acids (PUFAs) are involved both in immune system regulation and inflammation.
target: Polyunsaturated fatty acids (PUFAs) are involved both in immune system monitoring or regulation and infection-fighting processes such as inflammation.

source: In particular, within the PUFAs category, omega-3 (ω-3) may reduce inflammation, whereas omega-6 (ω-6) PUFAs are generally considered to have a proinflammatory effect.
target: In particular, within the PUFAs category, omega-3 (ω-3) may reduce inflammation, whereas omega-6 (ω-6) PUFAs are generally considered to have an inflammatory effect.

source: Recent evidence highlights an imbalance in the ω-3:ω-6 ratio with an increased intake of ω-6, as a consequence of the shift towards a westernized diet.
target: Recent evidence shows an imbalance in the ω-3:ω-6 ratio with an increased intake of ω-6, as a result of the shift towards a westernized diet.

source: In critical age groups such as infants, toddlers and young children, as well as pregnant and lactating women or fish allergic patients, ω-3 intake may be inadequate.
target: In important age groups such as infants, toddlers and young children, as well as pregnant and milk-giving women or fish allergic patients, ω-3 intake may be improper.

source: This review aims to discuss the potential beneficial effects of PUFAs on pediatric food allergy prevention and treatment, both at prenatal and postnatal ages.
target: This article aims to discuss the potential beneficial effects of PUFAs on child-related food allergy prevention and treatment, both at pre-birth and post-birth ages.

source: Data from preclinical studies with PUFAs supplementation show encouraging effects in suppressing allergic response.
target: Data from preclinical studies with PUFAs supplementation show encouraging effects in reducing allergic response.

source: Clinical studies results are still conflicting about the best timing and dosages of supplementation and which individuals are most likely to benefit; therefore, it is still not possible to draw firm conclusions.
target: Medical study results are still conflicting about the best timing and amounts of supplementation and which individuals are most likely to benefit. Therefore, it is still not possible to draw firm conclusions.

source: With regard to food-allergic children, it is still debated whether PUFAs could slow disease progression or not, since consistent data are lacking.
target: Regarding food-allergic children, it is still argued whether PUFAs could slow disease worsening or not, since consistent data are lacking.

source: In conclusion, more data on the effects of ω-3 PUFAs supplementation alone or in combination with other nutrients are warranted, both in the general and food allergic population.
target: In conclusion, more data on the effects of ω-3 PUFAs supplementation alone or in combination with other nutrients are needed, both in the general and food allergic population.

source: Elevated inflammatory cytokines and chronic pain are associated with shorter leukocyte telomere length (LTL), a measure of cellular aging.
target: Increased infection-fighting (inflammatory) molecules and lasting (chronic) pain are associated with shorter white blood cell gene end or telomere length (LTL), a measure of cellular aging.

source: Micronutrients, such as 25-hydroxyvitamin D (vitamin D) and omega 3, have anti-inflammatory properties.
target: Micronutrients, such as 25-hydroxyvitamin D (vitamin D) and omega 3, have anti-inflammatory properties.

source: Little is known regarding the relationships between vitamin D, omega 6:3 ratio, LTL, inflammation, and chronic pain.
target: Little is known about the relationships between vitamin D, omega 6:3 ratio, LTL, inflammation, and chronic pain.

source: We investigate associations between vitamin D, omega 6:3 ratio, LTL, and C-reactive protein (CRP) in people living with/without chronic pain overall and stratified by chronic pain status.
target: We investigate links between vitamin D, omega 6:3 ratio, LTL, and C-reactive protein (CRP, a marker of imflammation) in people living with/without chronic pain overall and grouped by chronic pain status.

source: A cross-sectional analysis of 402 individuals (63% women, 79.5% with chronic pain) was completed.
target: An analysis of 402 individuals (63% women, 79.5% with chronic pain) was done.

source: Demographic and health information was collected.
target: Basic and health information was collected.

source: Chronic pain was assessed as pain experienced for at least three months.
target: Chronic pain was measured as pain experienced for at least three months.

source: LTL was measured in genomic DNA isolated from blood leukocytes, and micronutrients and CRP were measured in serum samples.
target: LTL was measured in gene DNA isolated from blood white blood cells, and micronutrients and CRP were measured in blood samples.

source: Data were analyzed with general linear regression.
target: Data were analyzed with general mathematical analysis.

source: Although an association between the continuous micronutrients and LTL was not observed, a positive association between omega 6:3 ratio and CRP was detected.
target: Although a link between the continuous micronutrients and LTL was not found, a positive link between omega 6:3 ratio and CRP was detected.

source: In individuals with chronic pain, based on clinical categories, significant associations between vitamin D, omega 6:3 ratio, and CRP were observed.
target: In individuals with chronic pain, based on medical categories, significant links between vitamin D, omega 6:3 ratio, and CRP were observed.

source: Findings highlight the complex relationships between anti-inflammatory micronutrients, inflammation, cellular aging, and chronic pain.
target: Findings show the complex relationships between anti-inflammatory micronutrients, inflammation, cellular aging, and chronic pain.

source: Objective: The objective of this health technology policy analysis was to determine, where, how, and when physiotherapy services are best delivered to optimize functional outcomes for patients after they undergo primary (first-time) total hip replacement or total knee replacement, and to determine the Ontario-specific economic impact of the best delivery strategy.
target: The goal of this health technology policy analysis was to figure out, where, how, and when physiotherapy (PT) can best help patients after knee or hip replacement. We also hope to figure out the financial impact of the best PT strategies for Ontario.

source: The objectives of the systematic review were as follows: To determine the effectiveness of inpatient physiotherapy after discharge from an acute care hospital compared with outpatient physiotherapy delivered in either a clinic-based or home-based setting for primary total joint replacement patients.
target: Specifically, we wanted to figure out how inpatient PT (during a stay at a care facility) compared with outpatient PT, either at a clinic or at home, for patients that had joint replacements.

source: To determine the effectiveness of outpatient physiotherapy delivered by a physiotherapist in either a clinic-based or home-based setting in addition to a home exercise program compared with a home exercise program alone for primary total joint replacement patients.
target: We also wanted to see how effective outpatient PT with a therapist was, either at a clinic or at home, compared with just a home exercise program for patients that had joint replacements.

source: To determine the effectiveness of preoperative exercise for people who are scheduled to receive primary total knee or hip replacement surgery.
target: Finally, we aimed to figure out how effective it is to exercise before surgery for people who are scheduled for knee or hip replacement.

source: Conclusions: Based on the evidence, the Medical Advisory Secretariat reached the following conclusions with respect to physiotherapy rehabilitation and physical functioning 1 year after primary TKR or THR surgery: There is high-quality evidence from 1 large RCT to support the use of home-based physiotherapy instead of inpatient physiotherapy after primary THR or TKR surgery.
target: Based on the evidence, the Medical Advisory Secretariat concluded that there is good evidence to support using home-based PT instead of inpatient PT after hip or knee replacement. This was concluded with regard to function a year after the surgery.

source: There is low-to-moderate quality evidence from 1 large RCT to support the conclusion that receiving a monitoring phone call from a physiotherapist and practicing home exercises is comparable to receiving clinic-based physiotherapy and practicing home exercises for people who have had primary TKR surgery.
target: There is some evidence from one large study that getting phone call from a physiotherapist and practicing home exercises is comparable to having PT at a clinic and practicing home exercises for people who have had knee replacement.

source: However, results may not be generalizable to those who have had THR surgery.
target: However, the same might not apply to those who have hip replacement.

source: There is moderate evidence to suggest that an exercise program beginning 4 to 6 weeks before primary TKR surgery is not effective.
target: There is decent evidence that an exercise program beginning 4 to 6 weeks before knee replacement surgery is not effective.

source: Total knee arthroplasty (TKA) is the gold standard treatment for end-stage knee osteoarthritis.
target: Complete knee replacement surgery is the best known treatment for severe arthritis in the knee.

source: Most patients report successful long-term outcomes and reduced pain after TKA, but recovery is variable and the majority of patients continue to demonstrate lower extremity muscle weakness and functional deficits compared to age-matched control subjects.
target: Most patients have successful long-term outcomes and reduced pain after knee replacement. However, recovery varies, and most patients continue to have weakness in the legs and less function compared to peers of the same age.

source: Given the potential positive influence of postoperative rehabilitation and the lack of established standards for prescribing exercise paradigms after TKA, the purpose of this study was to systematically review randomized, controlled studies to determine the effectiveness of postoperative outpatient care on short- and long-term functional recovery.
target: There is a potential positive influence of rehab after surgery and a lack of established standards for prescribing exercise paradigms after knee replacement. With this in mind, our goal in this review article was to look carefully at studies to determine how effective outpatient care is for regaining function short-term and long-term.

source: Nineteen studies were identified as highly relevant for the review and four categories of postoperative intervention were discussed: 1) strengthening exercises; 2) aquatic therapy; 3) balance training; and 4) clinical environment.
target: We found 19 studies that we deemed very relevant for the review. We discussed four categories of treatment after surgery: 1) strengthening exercises; 2) aquatic therapy; 3) balance training; and 4) clinical environment.

source: Optimal outpatient physical therapy protocols should include: strengthening and intensive functional exercises given through land-based or aquatic programs, the intensity of which is increased based on patient progress.
target: The best outpatient physical therapy strategies should include strengthening and intensive functional exercises, either normal or in water. The intensity of these should increase based on patient progress.

source: Due to the highly individualized characteristics of these types of exercises, outpatient physical therapy performed in a clinic under the supervision of a trained physical therapist may provide the best long-term outcomes after the surgery.
target: Since these exercises are very individualized, outpatient physical therapy done in a clinic under with a trained physical therapist may provide the best long-term outcomes after the surgery.

source: Supervised or remotely supervised therapy may be effective at reducing some of the impairments following TKA, but several studies without direct oversight produced poor results.
target: Supervised or remotely supervised therapy may be effective at reducing some of the impairments following knee replacement, but several studies without direct oversight had poor results.

source: Most studies did not accurately describe the "usual care" or control groups and information about the dose, frequency, intensity and duration of the rehabilitation protocols were lacking from several studies.
target: Most studies did not accurately describe what “usual care” was used to compare against the programs they were testing. Information about the amount, frequency, intensity and duration of the rehab protocols was also missing from several studies.

source: Background: Rehabilitation, with an emphasis on physiotherapy and exercise, is widely promoted after total knee replacement.
target: Rehab, with an emphasis on physiotherapy and exercise, is widely promoted after total knee replacement.

source: However, provision of services varies in content and duration.
target: However, how services are given varies in content and duration.

source: The aim of this study is to update the review of Minns Lowe and colleagues 2007 using systematic review and meta-analysis to evaluate the effectiveness of post-discharge physiotherapy exercise in patients with primary total knee replacement.
target: The aim of this study is to provide an updated summary of the research to evaluate how effective physiotherapy (PT) exercise is for patients that had knee replacements.

source: Methods: We searched MEDLINE, Embase, PsycInfo, CINAHL and Cochrane CENTRAL to October 4(th) 2013 for randomised evaluations of physiotherapy exercise in adults with recent primary knee replacement.
target: We searched several medical literature databases, up to October 4th, 2013, for studies of PT exercise in adults that had knee replacements.

source: Outcomes were: patient-reported pain and function, knee range of motion, and functional performance.
target: The outcomes we looked at were: patient-reported pain and function, knee range of motion, and functional performance.

source: Authors were contacted for missing data and outcomes.
target: We contacted the researchers for missing data and outcomes.

source: Risk of bias and heterogeneity were assessed.
target: We also looked at whether the study results could be misleading.

source: Results: Searches identified 18 randomised trials including 1,739 patients with total knee replacement.
target: Our searches found 18 studies including 1,739 patients with total knee replacement.

source: Interventions compared: physiotherapy exercise and no provision; home and outpatient provision; pool and gym-based provision; walking skills and more general physiotherapy; and general physiotherapy exercise with and without additional balance exercises or ergometer cycling.
target: The treatments that were compared were: PT exercise without care; home and outpatient care; pool and gym-based care; walking skills and more general PT; and general PT exercise with and without additional balance exercises or a stationary bike.

source: Compared with controls receiving minimal physiotherapy, patients receiving physiotherapy exercise had improved physical function at 3-4 months, SMD -0.37 (95% CI -0.62, -0.12), and pain, SMD -0.45 (95% CI -0.85, -0.06).
target: Compared with people with little or no PT, patients that got PT exercise had improved physical function at 3-4 months.

source: Benefit up to 6 months was apparent when considering only higher quality studies.
target: When considering only higher quality studies, there was a clear benefit up to 6 months.

source: There were no differences for outpatient physiotherapy exercise compared with home-based provision in physical function or pain outcomes.
target: There were no differences for outpatient PT exercise compared with home-based care in physical function or pain outcomes.

source: There was a short-term benefit favouring home-based physiotherapy exercise for range of motion flexion.
target: There was a short-term benefit in favor of home-based PT exercise for range of motion.

source: There were no differences in outcomes when the comparator was hydrotherapy, or when additional balancing or cycling components were included.
target: There were no differences in outcomes for pool-based care, or when additional balancing or cycling components were included.

source: In one study, a walking skills intervention was associated with a long-term improvement in walking performance.
target: In one study, a walking skills treatment plan was associated with a long-term improvement in walking performance.

source: However, for all these evaluations studies were under-powered individually and in combination.
target: However, for all these evaluations, studies did not have enough patients to be sure of their significance, even taken altogether.
